text.DRB: (Let him not see the streams of the river, the torrents of honey, & of butter.)

text.NRSV: They will give back the fruit of their toil, and will not swallow it down; from the profit of their trading they will get no enjoyment.
text.Rotherham: In vain, he toiled, he shall not swallow, like wealth to be restored, in which he cannot exult!
text.KJV: That which he laboured for shall he restore, and shall not swallow it down: according to his substance shall the restitution be, and he shall not rejoice therein.
text.NABRE: He shall give back his gains, never used; like his profit from trade, never enjoyed.
text.DRB: He shall be punished for all things that he did, & yet not be consumed: according to the multitude of his inventions so also shall he suffer.

text.NRSV: For they have crushed and abandoned the poor, they have seized a house that they did not build.
text.Rotherham: For he hath oppressed—hath forsaken the poor, A house, hath he seized, which he cannot rebuild.
text.KJV: Because he hath oppressed and hath forsaken the poor; because he hath violently taken away an house which he builded not;
text.NABRE: Because he has oppressed and neglected the poor, and stolen a house he did not build;
text.DRB: Because breaking in he hath made the poor naked: he hath violently taken a house, & built it not.

text.NRSV: “Because they knew no quiet in their bellies, in their greed they let nothing escape.
text.Rotherham: Surely he hath known no peace in his inmost mind,—With his dearest thing, shall he not get away:
text.KJV: Surely he shall not feel quietness in his belly, he shall not save of that which he desired.
text.NABRE: For he has known no quiet in his greed, in his treasure he cannot save himself.
text.DRB: Neither is his belly filled: and when he shall have the things he coveted, he cannot possess them.

text.NRSV: There was nothing left after they had eaten; therefore their prosperity will not endure.
text.Rotherham: Nothing escaped his devouring greed,—For this cause, shall his prosperity not continue:
text.KJV: There shall none of his meat be left; therefore shall no man look for his goods.
text.NABRE: None of his survivors will consume it, therefore his prosperity shall not endure.
text.DRB: There remained not of his meat, & therefore nothing shall continue of his goods:

text.NRSV: In full sufficiency they will be in distress; all the force of misery will come upon them.
text.Rotherham: When his abundance is gone, he shall be in straits, All the power of distress, shall come upon him.
text.KJV: In the fulness of his sufficiency he shall be in straits: every hand of the wicked shall come upon him.
text.NABRE: When he has more than enough, distress shall be his, every sort of trouble shall come upon him.
text.DRB: When he shall be filled, he shall be straitened, he shall burn, and all sorrow shall fall upon him.

text.NRSV: To fill their belly to the full God will send his fierce anger into them, and rain it upon them as their food.
text.Rotherham: It shall be that, to fill his belly, he will thrust at him the glow of his anger, and rain [it] upon him for his punishment.
text.KJV: When he is about to fill his belly, God shall cast the fury of his wrath upon him, and shall rain it upon him while he is eating.
text.NABRE: When he has filled his belly, God shall send against him the fury of his wrath and rain down his missiles upon him.
text.DRB: Would God his belly were filled, that he may send forth the wrath of his fury upon him, and rain his battle upon him.

text.NRSV: They will flee from an iron weapon; a bronze bow will strike them through.
text.Rotherham: He shall flee from the armour of iron,—There shall pierce him, a bow of bronze!
text.KJV: He shall flee from the iron weapon, and the bow of steel shall strike him through.
text.NABRE: Should he escape an iron weapon, a bronze bow shall pierce him through;
text.DRB: He shall fly weapons of iron, and shall fall upon a bow of brass.

text.NRSV: It is drawn forth and comes out of their body, the glittering point comes out of their gall; terrors come upon them.
text.Rotherham: He hath drawn it out, and it hath come forth out of his back,—yea the flashing arrow-head, out of his gall, There shall march on him—terrors:
text.KJV: It is drawn, and cometh out of the body; yea, the glittering sword cometh out of his gall: terrors are upon him.
text.NABRE: The dart shall come out of his back, a shining point out of his gall-bladder: terrors fall upon him.
text.DRB: The sword plucked out, and coming forth of his scabbard, and glistering in his bitterness: the horrible shall go and come upon him.

text.NRSV: Utter darkness is laid up for their treasures; a fire unfanned will consume them; it will devour what is left in their tent.
text.Rotherham: Every misfortune, is laid up for his treasures,—There shall consume, a fire, not blown up,—it shall destroy what remaineth in his tent:
text.KJV: All darkness shall be hid in his secret places: a fire not blown shall consume him; it shall go ill with him that is left in his tabernacle.
text.NABRE: Complete darkness is in store for his treasured ones; a fire unfanned shall consume him; any survivor in his tent shall be destroyed.
text.DRB: All darkness is hid in his secrets: fire that is not kindled shall devour him, he shall be afflicted left in his tabernacle.

text.NRSV: The heavens will reveal their iniquity, and the earth will rise up against them.
text.Rotherham: The heavens shall reveal his iniquity, and, the earth, be rising up against him:
text.KJV: The heaven shall reveal his iniquity; and the earth shall rise up against him.
text.NABRE: The heavens shall reveal his guilt, and the earth rise up against him.
text.DRB: The heavens shall reveal his iniquity, and the earth shall rise against him.

text.NRSV: The possessions of their house will be carried away, a flood will sweep them away on the day of God’s wrath.
text.Rotherham: The increase of his house shall vanish, melting away in the day of his anger.
text.KJV: The increase of his house shall depart, and his goods shall flow away in the day of his wrath.
text.NABRE: The flood shall sweep away his house, torrents in the day of God’s anger.
text.DRB: The blossom of his house shall be opened, he shall be plucked down in the day of God's fury.

text.NRSV: This is the portion of the wicked from God, the heritage appointed for them by God.”
text.Rotherham: This, is the portion of the lawless man, from God, and the inheritance decreed him from the Mighty One.
text.KJV: This is the portion of a wicked man from God, and the heritage appointed unto him by God.
text.NABRE: This is the portion of the wicked, the heritage appointed him by God.
text.DRB: This is the portion of an impious man from God, & the inheritance of his words from our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Job answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Job, and said:—
text.KJV: But Job answered and said,
text.NABRE: Then Job answered and said:
text.DRB: But Job answering, said:

text.NRSV: “Listen carefully to my words, and let this be your consolation.
text.Rotherham: Hear ye patiently my words, and let this be your consolation:
text.KJV: Hear diligently my speech, and let this be your consolations.
text.NABRE: At least listen to my words, and let that be the consolation you offer.
text.DRB: Hear I beseech you my words, and do penance.

text.NRSV: Bear with me, and I will speak; then after I have spoken, mock on.
text.Rotherham: Suffer me, that, I, may speak, and, after I have spoken, thou canst mock!
text.KJV: Suffer me that I may speak; and after that I have spoken, mock on.
text.NABRE: Bear with me while I speak; and after I have spoken, you can mock!
text.DRB: Bear with me, that I also may speak, and after my words, if it shall seem good, laugh ye.

text.NRSV: As for me, is my complaint addressed to mortals? Why should I not be impatient?
text.Rotherham: Did, I, unto man, make my complaint? Wherefore, then, should my spirit not be impatient?
text.KJV: As for me, is my complaint to man? and if it were so, why should not my spirit be troubled?
text.NABRE: Is my complaint toward any human being? Why should I not be impatient?
text.DRB: Is my disputation against man, that I ought not worthily to be sorry?

text.NRSV: Look at me, and be appalled, and lay your hand upon your mouth.
text.Rotherham: Turn round to me, and be astonished, and lay hand on mouth!
text.KJV: Mark me, and be astonished, and lay your hand upon your mouth.
text.NABRE: Look at me and be appalled, put your hands over your mouths.
text.DRB: Harken to me, and be astonished, and put the finger upon your mouth.

text.NRSV: When I think of it I am dismayed, and shuddering seizes my flesh.
text.Rotherham: When I call to mind, then am I dismayed, and there seizeth my flesh a shuddering:—
text.KJV: Even when I remember I am afraid, and trembling taketh hold on my flesh.
text.NABRE: When I think of it, I am dismayed, and shuddering seizes my flesh.
text.DRB: And I when I shall remember, am afraid, and trembling shaketh my flesh.

text.NRSV: Why do the wicked live on, reach old age, and grow mighty in power?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore do, lawless men, live, advance in years, even wax mighty in power?
text.KJV: Wherefore do the wicked live, become old, yea, are mighty in power?
text.NABRE: Why do the wicked keep on living, grow old, become mighty in power?
text.DRB: Why then do the impious live, are they advanced, & strengthened with riches?

text.NRSV: Their offspring are established in their presence, and their descendants before their eyes.
text.Rotherham: Their seed, is established in their sight, along with them, yea their offspring, before their eyes;
text.KJV: Their seed is established in their sight with them, and their offspring before their eyes.
text.NABRE: Their progeny is secure in their sight; their offspring are before their eyes.
text.DRB: Their seed continueth before them, a multitude of kinsmen, & of nephews in their sight.

text.NRSV: Their houses are safe from fear, and no rod of God is upon them.
text.Rotherham: Their houses, are at peace, without dread, neither is, the rod of GOD, upon them;
text.KJV: Their houses are safe from fear, neither is the rod of God upon them.
text.NABRE: Their homes are safe, without fear, and the rod of God is not upon them.
text.DRB: Their houses be secure & peaceable, & the rod of God is not upon them.

text.NRSV: Their bull breeds without fail; their cow calves and never miscarries.
text.Rotherham: His bull, covereth, and causeth not aversion, His cow safely calveth, and casteth not her young;
text.KJV: Their bull gendereth, and faileth not; their cow calveth, and casteth not her calf.
text.NABRE: Their bulls breed without fail; their cows calve and do not miscarry.
text.DRB: Their bullock hath conceived, & hath not made abortion: their cow hath calved, and is not deprived of her calf.

text.NRSV: They send out their little ones like a flock, and their children dance.
text.Rotherham: They send forth—like a flock—their young ones, and, their children, skip about for joy;
text.KJV: They send forth their little ones like a flock, and their children dance.
text.NABRE: They let their young run free like sheep, their children skip about.
text.DRB: Their little ones go forth as flocks, and their infants rejoice with pastimes.

text.NRSV: They sing to the tambourine and lyre, and rejoice to the sound of the pipe.
text.Rotherham: They rejoice aloud as [with] timbrel and lyre, and make merry to the sound of the pipe;
text.KJV: They take the timbrel and harp, and rejoice at the sound of the organ.
text.NABRE: They sing along with drum and lyre, and make merry to the sound of the pipe.
text.DRB: They hold the timbrel, & the harp, & rejoice at the sound of the organ.

text.NRSV: They spend their days in prosperity, and in peace they go down to Sheol.
text.Rotherham: They complete, in prosperity, their days, and, in a moment to hades, they sink down.
text.KJV: They spend their days in wealth, and in a moment go down to the grave.
text.NABRE: They live out their days in prosperity, and tranquilly go down to Sheol.
text.DRB: They lead their days in wealth, and in a moment they go down to hell.

text.NRSV: They say to God, ‘Leave us alone! We do not desire to know your ways.
text.Rotherham: Yet they said unto GOD, Depart from us, and, In the knowledge of thy ways, find we no pleasure.
text.KJV: Therefore they say unto God, Depart from us; for we desire not the knowledge of thy ways.
text.NABRE: Yet they say to God, “Depart from us, for we have no desire to know your ways!
text.DRB: Who said to God: depart from us, we will not the knowledge of thy ways.

text.NRSV: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? And what profit do we get if we pray to him?’
text.Rotherham: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? Or what shall we profit, that we should urge him?
text.KJV: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? and what profit should we have, if we pray unto him?
text.NABRE: What is the Almighty that we should serve him? And what do we gain by praying to him?”
text.DRB: Who is the Omnipotent, that we should serve him? And what doth it profit us if we shall pray him?

text.NRSV: Is not their prosperity indeed their own achievement? The plans of the wicked are repugnant to me.
text.Rotherham: Lo! not in their own hand, is their welfare, The counsel of lawless men, is far from me!
text.KJV: Lo, their good is not in their hand: the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
text.NABRE: Their happiness is not in their own hands. The designs of the wicked are far from me!
text.DRB: But yet because their good things are not in their hand, be the counsel of the impious far from me.

text.NRSV: “How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? How often does calamity come upon them? How often does God distribute pains in his anger?
text.Rotherham: How oft, the lamp of the lawless, goeth out, and their calamity, cometh upon them, Sorrows, apportioneth he in his anger;
text.KJV: How oft is the candle of the wicked put out! and how oft cometh their destruction upon them! God distributeth sorrows in his anger.
text.NABRE: How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? How often does destruction come upon them, the portion God allots in his anger?
text.DRB: How often shall the candle of the impious be extinguished, and inundation come upon them, and shall he divide the sorrows of his fury?

text.NRSV: How often are they like straw before the wind, and like chaff that the storm carries away?
text.Rotherham: They become as straw before the wind, and as chaff, which the storm stealeth away.
text.KJV: They are as stubble before the wind, and as chaff that the storm carrieth away.
text.NABRE: Let them be like straw before the wind, like chaff the storm carries away!
text.DRB: They shall be as chaff before the face of the wind, and as ashes, which the whirlwind scattereth.

text.NRSV: You say, ‘God stores up their iniquity for their children.’ Let it be paid out to them and let them know it.
text.Rotherham: Shall, GOD, reserve, for his children, his sorrow? Let him recompense him so that he may know it;
text.KJV: God layeth up his iniquity for his children: he rewardeth him, and he shall know it.
text.NABRE: “God is storing up the man’s misery for his children”?— let him requite the man himself so that he knows it!
text.DRB: God shall reserve the sorrow of the father to his children: and when he shall have rendered it, then shall he know.

text.NRSV: Let their own eyes see their destruction, and let them drink of the wrath of the Almighty.
text.Rotherham: His own eyes, shall see his misfortune, and, the wrath of the Almighty, shall he drink.
text.KJV: His eyes shall see his destruction, and he shall drink of the wrath of the Almighty.
text.NABRE: Let his own eyes behold his calamity, and the wrath of the Almighty let him drink!
text.DRB: His eyes shall see his own slaughter, and he shall drink the fury of the Omnipotent.

text.NRSV: For what do they care for their household after them, when the number of their months is cut off?
text.Rotherham: For what shall be his pleasure in his house after him, when, the number of his months, is cut in twain?
text.KJV: For what pleasure hath he in his house after him, when the number of his months is cut off in the midst?
text.NABRE: For what interest has he in his family after him, when the number of his months is finished?
text.DRB: For what doeth it pertain to him concerning his house after him: although the number of his months be diminished the half?

text.NRSV: Will any teach God knowledge, seeing that he judges those that are on high?
text.Rotherham: Is it, to GOD, one can teach knowledge, seeing that, he, shall judge, them who are on high?
text.KJV: Shall any teach God knowledge? seeing he judgeth those that are high.
text.NABRE: Can anyone teach God knowledge, seeing that he judges those on high?
text.DRB: Shall any man teach God knowledge, who judgeth the high ones?

text.NRSV: One dies in full prosperity, being wholly at ease and secure,
text.Rotherham: This, man dieth, in the very perfection of his prosperity, wholly tranquil and secure;
text.KJV: One dieth in his full strength, being wholly at ease and quiet.
text.NABRE: One dies in his full vigor, wholly at ease and content;
text.DRB: This man dieth strong and in health, rich and happy.

text.NRSV: his loins full of milk and the marrow of his bones moist.
text.Rotherham: His veins, are filled with nourishment, and, the marrow of his bones, is fresh;
text.KJV: His breasts are full of milk, and his bones are moistened with marrow.
text.NABRE: His figure is full and nourished, his bones are moist with marrow.
text.DRB: His bowels be full of fat, and his bones be embrued with marrow.

text.NRSV: Another dies in bitterness of soul, never having tasted of good.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, this other man, dieth, in bitterness of soul, and hath never tasted good fortune:
text.KJV: And another dieth in the bitterness of his soul, and never eateth with pleasure.
text.NABRE: Another dies with a bitter spirit, never having tasted happiness.
text.DRB: But another dieth in bitterness of soul without any riches:

text.NRSV: They lie down alike in the dust, and the worms cover them.
text.Rotherham: Together, in the dust, they lie down, and, the worm, spreadeth a covering over them.
text.KJV: They shall lie down alike in the dust, and the worms shall cover them.
text.NABRE: Alike they lie down in the dust, and worms cover them both.
text.DRB: And yet they shall sleep together in the dust, and worms shall cover them.

text.NRSV: “Oh, I know your thoughts, and your schemes to wrong me.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I know your plans, and the devices, wherewith ye would do me violence!
text.KJV: Behold, I know your thoughts, and the devices which ye wrongfully imagine against me.
text.NABRE: See, I know your thoughts, and the arguments you plot against me.
text.DRB: Surely I know your cogitations, & unjust sentences against me.

text.NRSV: For you say, ‘Where is the house of the prince? Where is the tent in which the wicked lived?’
text.Rotherham: For ye say, Where is the house of the noble-minded? And where the dwelling-tent of the lawless?
text.KJV: For ye say, Where is the house of the prince? and where are the dwelling places of the wicked?
text.NABRE: For you say, “Where is the house of the great, and where the dwelling place of the wicked?”
text.DRB: For you say: Where is the house of the Prince? & where are the tabernacles of the impious?

text.NRSV: Have you not asked those who travel the roads, and do you not accept their testimony,
text.Rotherham: Have ye not asked the passers-by in the way? And, their signs, can ye not recognise?
text.KJV: Have ye not asked them that go by the way? and do ye not know their tokens,
text.NABRE: Have you not asked the wayfarers and do you not acknowledge the witness they give?
text.DRB: Ask any of the wayfaring men, & you shall understand that he knoweth these self-same things.

text.NRSV: that the wicked are spared in the day of calamity, and are rescued in the day of wrath?
text.Rotherham: That, to the day of calamity, is the wicked reserved, to the day of indignant visitation, are they led.
text.KJV: That the wicked is reserved to the day of destruction? they shall be brought forth to the day of wrath.
text.NABRE: On the day of calamity the evil man is spared, on the day that wrath is released.
text.DRB: Because the evil man is kept unto the day of perdition, and he shall be led to the day of fury.

text.NRSV: Who declares their way to their face, and who repays them for what they have done?
text.Rotherham: Who can declare—to his face—his way? And, what, he, hath done, who shall recompense to him?
text.KJV: Who shall declare his way to his face? and who shall repay him what he hath done?
text.NABRE: Who will charge him to his face about his conduct, and for what he has done who will repay him?
text.DRB: Who shall reprove his way before him? And who shall repay him the things that he hath done?

text.NRSV: When they are carried to the grave, a watch is kept over their tomb.
text.Rotherham: Yet, he, to the graves, is borne, and, over the tomb, one keepeth watch;
text.KJV: Yet shall he be brought to the grave, and shall remain in the tomb.
text.NABRE: He is carried to the grave and at his tomb they keep watch.
text.DRB: He shall be brought to the graves, and shall watch in the head of the dead.

text.NRSV: The clods of the wadi are sweet to them; all people will follow after, and those who went before them are innumerable.
text.Rotherham: Pleasant to him are the mounds of the torrent-bed,—and, after him, doth every man march, as, before him, there were without number.
text.KJV: The clods of the valley shall be sweet unto him, and every man shall draw after him, as there are innumerable before him.
text.NABRE: Sweet to him are the clods of the valley. All humankind will follow after him, and countless others before him.
text.DRB: He hath been sweet to the gravel of Cocytus, and after him he shall draw every man, and before him innumerable.

text.NRSV: How then will you comfort me with empty nothings? There is nothing left of your answers but falsehood.”
text.Rotherham: How then should ye comfort me with vanity, since, as for your replies, there lurketh, [in them] treachery?
text.KJV: How then comfort ye me in vain, seeing in your answers there remaineth falsehood?
text.NABRE: How empty the consolation you offer me! Your arguments remain a fraud.
text.DRB: How therefore do ye comfort me in vain, whereas your answer is shewed to be repugnant to the truth?

text.NRSV: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Eliphaz the Temanite, and said:—
text.KJV: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,
text.NABRE: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
text.DRB: But Eliphaz the Themanite answering, said:

text.NRSV: “Can a mortal be of use to God? Can even the wisest be of service to him?
text.Rotherham: Unto GOD, can a man act as friend? Surely a discreet man befriendeth himself!
text.KJV: Can a man be profitable unto God, as he that is wise may be profitable unto himself?
text.NABRE: Can a man be profitable to God? Can a wise man be profitable to him?
text.DRB: Can man be compared with God, yea though he be of perfect knowledge?

text.NRSV: Is it any pleasure to the Almighty if you are righteous, or is it gain to him if you make your ways blameless?
text.Rotherham: Is it a pleasure to the Almighty, that thou shouldst be righteous? or any profit, that thou shouldst be blameless in thy ways?
text.KJV: Is it any pleasure to the Almighty, that thou art righteous? or is it gain to him, that thou makest thy ways perfect?
text.NABRE: Does it please the Almighty that you are just? Does he gain if your ways are perfect?
text.DRB: What doth it profit God if thou be just? Or what doest thou advantage him if thy way be unspotted?

text.NRSV: Is it for your piety that he reproves you, and enters into judgment with you?
text.Rotherham: Is it, for thy reverence, that he will accuse thee? will enter with thee into judgment?
text.KJV: Will he reprove thee for fear of thee? will he enter with thee into judgment?
text.NABRE: Is it because of your piety that he reproves you— that he enters into judgment with you?
text.DRB: Shall he be afraid to reprove thee, and come with thee into judgment:

text.NRSV: Is not your wickedness great? There is no end to your iniquities.
text.Rotherham: Is not, thy wickedness, great? and, without end, [are not] thine iniquities?
text.KJV: Is not thy wickedness great? and thine iniquities infinite?
text.NABRE: Is not your wickedness great, your iniquity endless?
text.DRB: And not for thy very great malice, and thine infinite iniquities?

text.NRSV: For you have exacted pledges from your family for no reason, and stripped the naked of their clothing.
text.Rotherham: Surely then hast been wont to put thy brother in pledge, for nothing, and, the garments of the ill-clad, hast thou stripped off:
text.KJV: For thou hast taken a pledge from thy brother for nought, and stripped the naked of their clothing.
text.NABRE: You keep your relatives’ goods in pledge unjustly, leave them stripped naked of their clothing.
text.DRB: For thou hast taken away the pledge of thy brethren without cause, and the naked thou hast spoiled of cloths.

text.NRSV: You have given no water to the weary to drink, and you have withheld food from the hungry.
text.Rotherham: No water—to the weary, hast thou given to drink, and, from the hungry, thou hast withheld bread:
text.KJV: Thou hast not given water to the weary to drink, and thou hast withholden bread from the hungry.
text.NABRE: To the thirsty you give no water to drink, and from the hungry you withhold bread;
text.DRB: Water to the weary thou hast not given, and from the hungry thou hast withdrawn bread.

text.NRSV: The powerful possess the land, and the favored live in it.
text.Rotherham: A man of might, to him, pertaineth the land, and, the favorite, dwelleth therein:
text.KJV: But as for the mighty man, he had the earth; and the honourable man dwelt in it.
text.NABRE: As if the land belonged to the powerful, and only the privileged could dwell in it!
text.DRB: In the strength of thine arm thou didst possess the earth, and being the mightiest thou didst obtain it.

text.NRSV: You have sent widows away empty-handed, and the arms of the orphans you have crushed.
text.Rotherham: Widows, thou hast sent away empty, and, the arms of the fatherless, thou dost crush.
text.KJV: Thou hast sent widows away empty, and the arms of the fatherless have been broken.
text.NABRE: You sent widows away empty-handed, and the resources of orphans are destroyed.
text.DRB: Widows thou hast sent away empty, and the arms of pupils thou hast broken in pieces.

text.NRSV: Therefore snares are around you, and sudden terror overwhelms you,
text.Rotherham: For this cause, round about thee, are snares, and a dread startleth thee suddenly;
text.KJV: Therefore snares are round about thee, and sudden fear troubleth thee;
text.NABRE: Therefore snares are round about you, sudden terror makes you panic,
text.DRB: Therefore art thou compassed with snares, and sudden fear troubleth thee.

text.NRSV: or darkness so that you cannot see; a flood of water covers you.
text.Rotherham: Or darkness—thou canst not see, and, a flood of waters, covereth thee.
text.KJV: Or darkness, that thou canst not see; and abundance of waters cover thee.
text.NABRE: Or darkness—you cannot see! A deluge of waters covers you.
text.DRB: And thoughtest thou that thou shouldest not see darkness, and that thou shouldest not be oppressed with the violence of overflowing waters?

text.NRSV: “Is not God high in the heavens? See the highest stars, how lofty they are!
text.Rotherham: Is not, GOD, [in] the height of the heavens? Behold, then, the head of the stars, that they are high.
text.KJV: Is not God in the height of heaven? and behold the height of the stars, how high they are!
text.NABRE: Does not God, in the heights of the heavens, behold the top of the stars, high though they are?
text.DRB: Doest thou not think that God is higher than heaven, and is exalted above the top of the stars?

text.NRSV: Therefore you say, ‘What does God know? Can he judge through the deep darkness?
text.Rotherham: Wilt thou say then, What doth GOD know? Out through a thick cloud, can he judge?
text.KJV: And thou sayest, How doth God know? can he judge through the dark cloud?
text.NABRE: Yet you say, “What does God know? Can he judge through the thick darkness?
text.DRB: And thou sayst: For what knoweth God? And he judgeth as it were by a mist.

text.NRSV: Thick clouds enwrap him, so that he does not see, and he walks on the dome of heaven.’
text.Rotherham: Dark clouds, are a veil to him, and he cannot see, or, the vault of the heavens, doth he walk?
text.KJV: Thick clouds are a covering to him, that he seeth not; and he walketh in the circuit of heaven.
text.NABRE: Clouds hide him so that he cannot see as he walks around the circuit of the heavens!”
text.DRB: The clouds are his covert, neither doth he consider our things, and he walketh about the poles of heaven.

text.NRSV: Will you keep to the old way that the wicked have trod?
text.Rotherham: The path of the ancient time, wilt thou mark, which the men of iniquity trod?
text.KJV: Hast thou marked the old way which wicked men have trodden?
text.NABRE: Do you indeed keep to the ancient way trodden by the worthless?
text.DRB: Doest thou covet to keep the path of worlds, which wicked men have trodden?

text.NRSV: They were snatched away before their time; their foundation was washed away by a flood.
text.Rotherham: Who were snatched away before the time, and, a stream, washed away their foundation?
text.KJV: Which were cut down out of time, whose foundation was overflown with a flood:
text.NABRE: They were snatched before their time; their foundations a river swept away.
text.DRB: Who were taken away before their time, and a flood hath overthrown their foundation.

text.NRSV: They said to God, ‘Leave us alone,’ and ‘What can the Almighty do to us?’
text.Rotherham: Who had been saying unto GOD, Depart from us! and—What can the Almighty do for himself?
text.KJV: Which said unto God, Depart from us: and what can the Almighty do for them?
text.NABRE: They said to God, “Let us alone!” and, “What can the Almighty do to us?”
text.DRB: Who said to God: Depart from us; and as though the Omnipotent could do nothing, they esteemed him:

text.NRSV: Yet he filled their houses with good things—but the plans of the wicked are repugnant to me.
text.Rotherham: Yet, he, had filled their houses with good! The counsel of the lawless, then, is far from me:
text.KJV: Yet he filled their houses with good things: but the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
text.NABRE: Yet he had filled their houses with good things. The designs of the wicked are far from me!
text.DRB: Whereas he had filled their houses with good things, whose sentence be far from me.

text.NRSV: The righteous see it and are glad; the innocent laugh them to scorn, saying,
text.Rotherham: The righteous shall see and rejoice, and, the innocent, shall laugh them to scorn:
text.KJV: The righteous see it, and are glad: and the innocent laugh them to scorn.
text.NABRE: The just look on and are glad, and the innocent deride them:
text.DRB: The just shall see, & shall rejoice, & the innocent man shall scorn them.

text.NRSV: ‘Surely our adversaries are cut off, and what they left the fire has consumed.’
text.Rotherham: If our assailants do not vanish, then, their abundance, a fire consumeth!
text.KJV: Whereas our substance is not cut down, but the remnant of them the fire consumeth.
text.NABRE: “Truly our enemies are destroyed, and what was left to them, fire has consumed!”
text.DRB: Is not their exaltation cut down, and hath not fire devoured the remnants of them?

text.NRSV: “Agree with God, and be at peace; in this way good will come to you.
text.Rotherham: Shew thyself to be one with him—I pray thee—and prosper, thereby, shall there come on thee blessing.
text.KJV: Acquaint now thyself with him, and be at peace: thereby good shall come unto thee.
text.NABRE: Settle with him and have peace. That way good shall come to you:
text.DRB: Agree thou therefore to him, and have peace: and by these things thou shalt have the best fruits.

text.NRSV: Receive instruction from his mouth, and lay up his words in your heart.
text.Rotherham: Accept, I beseech thee, from his mouth—instruction,—and lay up his sayings in thy heart.
text.KJV: Receive, I pray thee, the law from his mouth, and lay up his words in thine heart.
text.NABRE: Receive instruction from his mouth, and place his words in your heart.
text.DRB: Receive the law of his mouth, and put his words in thy heart.

text.NRSV: If you return to the Almighty, you will be restored, if you remove unrighteousness from your tents,
text.Rotherham: If thou return unto the Almighty and submit thyself, if thou far remove perversity from thy tent,
text.KJV: If thou return to the Almighty, thou shalt be built up, thou shalt put away iniquity far from thy tabernacles.
text.NABRE: If you return to the Almighty, you will be restored; if you put iniquity far from your tent,
text.DRB: If thou wilt return to the Omnipotent, thou shalt be builded up, and shalt make iniquity far from thy tabernacle.

text.NRSV: if you treat gold like dust, and gold of Ophir like the stones of the wadi,
text.Rotherham: Then lay up, in the dust, precious ore, and, among the stones of the torrent-beds, fine gold:
text.KJV: Then shalt thou lay up gold as dust, and the gold of Ophir as the stones of the brooks.
text.NABRE: And treat raw gold as dust, the fine gold of Ophir as pebbles in the wadi,
text.DRB: He shall give for earth flint, and for flint torrents of gold.

text.NRSV: and if the Almighty is your gold and your chosen silver,
text.Rotherham: So shall, the Almighty, become, thy precious ores, yea glittering silver unto thee!
text.KJV: Yea, the Almighty shall be thy defence, and thou shalt have plenty of silver.
text.NABRE: Then the Almighty himself shall be your gold and your sparkling silver.
text.DRB: And the Omnipotent shall be against thine enemies, and silver shall be heaped together unto thee.

text.NRSV: then you will delight yourself in the Almighty, and lift up your face to God.
text.Rotherham: For, then, in the Almighty, shalt thou take exquisite delight, and shalt lift up—unto GOD—thy face;
text.KJV: For then shalt thou have thy delight in the Almighty, and shalt lift up thy face unto God.
text.NABRE: For then you shall delight in the Almighty, you shall lift up your face toward God.
text.DRB: Then shalt thou abound in delights upon the Omnipotent, and shalt lift up thy face to God.

text.NRSV: You will pray to him, and he will hear you, and you will pay your vows.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt make entreaty unto him, and he will hear thee, and, thy vows, shalt thou pay;
text.KJV: Thou shalt make thy prayer unto him, and he shall hear thee, and thou shalt pay thy vows.
text.NABRE: Entreat him and he will hear you, and your vows you shall fulfill.
text.DRB: Thou shalt ask him, and he will hear thee, and thou shalt pay thy vows.

text.NRSV: You will decide on a matter, and it will be established for you, and light will shine on your ways.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt decree a purpose, and it shall be fulfilled unto thee, and, upon thy ways, shall have shone a light;
text.KJV: Thou shalt also decree a thing, and it shall be established unto thee: and the light shall shine upon thy ways.
text.NABRE: What you decide shall succeed for you, and upon your ways light shall shine.
text.DRB: Thou shalt decree a thing, and it shall come to thee, and light shall shine in thy ways.

text.NRSV: For God abases the proud, but he saves the lowly.
text.Rotherham: When men cast themselves down, then thou shalt say: Up! And, him that is of downcast eyes, shall he save;
text.KJV: When men are cast down, then thou shalt say, There is lifting up; and he shall save the humble person.
text.NABRE: For when they are brought low, you will say, “It is pride!” But downcast eyes he saves.
text.DRB: For who will be humbled, shall be in glory: & he that will bow down his eyes, he shall be saved.

text.NRSV: He will deliver even those who are not innocent; they will be delivered through the cleanness of your hands.”
text.Rotherham: He shall deliver the innocent, and thou shalt escape by the pureness of thy hands.
text.KJV: He shall deliver the island of the innocent: and it is delivered by the pureness of thine hands.
text.NABRE: He will deliver whoever is innocent; you shall be delivered if your hands are clean.
text.DRB: The innocent shall be saved, but shall be saved in the cleanness of his hands.

text.NRSV: Then Job answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Job, and said:—
text.KJV: Then Job answered and said,
text.NABRE: Then Job answered and said:
text.DRB: But Job answering, said:

text.NRSV: “Today also my complaint is bitter; his hand is heavy despite my groaning.
text.Rotherham: Even to-day, is my complaint rebellion? His hand, is heavier than my groaning.
text.KJV: Even to day is my complaint bitter: my stroke is heavier than my groaning.
text.NABRE: Today especially my complaint is bitter, his hand is heavy upon me in my groanings.
text.DRB: Now also my talk is in bitterness, and the hand of my plague is aggravated upon my mourning.

text.NRSV: O that I knew where I might find him, that I might come even to his dwelling!
text.Rotherham: Oh that I knew where I might find him! I would come even unto his dwelling-place;
text.KJV: Oh that I knew where I might find him! that I might come even to his seat!
text.NABRE: Would that I knew how to find him, that I might come to his dwelling!
text.DRB: Who will grant me that I may know and find him, & come even to his throne?

text.NRSV: I would lay my case before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.
text.Rotherham: I would set out, before him, a plea, and, my mouth, would I fill with arguments;
text.KJV: I would order my cause before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.
text.NABRE: I would set out my case before him, fill my mouth with arguments;
text.DRB: I will set judgement before him, and will fill my mouth with accusations.

text.NRSV: I would learn what he would answer me, and understand what he would say to me.
text.Rotherham: I would note the words wherewith he would respond to me, and would mark what he would say to me.
text.KJV: I would know the words which he would answer me, and understand what he would say unto me.
text.NABRE: I would learn the words he would answer me, understand what he would say to me.
text.DRB: That I may know the words that he will answer me, and understand what he will speak to me.

text.NRSV: Would he contend with me in the greatness of his power? No; but he would give heed to me.
text.Rotherham: Would he, with fulness of might, contend with me? Nay, surely, he, would give heed to me!
text.KJV: Will he plead against me with his great power? No; but he would put strength in me.
text.NABRE: Would he contend against me with his great power? No, he himself would heed me!
text.DRB: I will not that he contend with me with much strength, nor that he oppress me with the weight of his greatness.

text.NRSV: There an upright person could reason with him, and I should be acquitted forever by my judge.
text.Rotherham: There an upright man, might reason with him, so should I deliver myself completely from my judge.
text.KJV: There the righteous might dispute with him; so should I be delivered for ever from my judge.
text.NABRE: There an upright man might argue with him, and I would once and for all be delivered from my judge.
text.DRB: Let him propose equity against me, and my judgment shall come to victory.

text.NRSV: “If I go forward, he is not there; or backward, I cannot perceive him;
text.Rotherham: Behold! eastward, I go, but he is not there, and, westward, but I perceive him not;
text.KJV: Behold, I go forward, but he is not there; and backward, but I cannot perceive him:
text.NABRE: But if I go east, he is not there; or west, I cannot perceive him;
text.DRB: If I shall go to the East, he appeareth not: if to the West, I shall not understand him.

text.NRSV: on the left he hides, and I cannot behold him; I turn to the right, and I cannot see him.
text.Rotherham: On the north, where he worketh, but I get no vision, He hideth himself on the south that I cannot see him.
text.KJV: On the left hand, where he doth work, but I cannot behold him: he hideth himself on the right hand, that I cannot see him:
text.NABRE: The north enfolds him, and I cannot catch sight of him; The south hides him, and I cannot see him.
text.DRB: If to the left hand, what shall I do? I shall not apprehend him: if I turn myself to the right hand, I shall not see him.

text.NRSV: But he knows the way that I take; when he has tested me, I shall come out like gold.
text.Rotherham: But, he, knoweth the way that I choose, Having tried me, as gold, I shall come forth.
text.KJV: But he knoweth the way that I take: when he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold.
text.NABRE: Yet he knows my way; if he tested me, I should come forth like gold.
text.DRB: But he knoweth my way, and hath proved me as gold that passeth through the fire:

text.NRSV: My foot has held fast to his steps; I have kept his way and have not turned aside.
text.Rotherham: Of his steps, my foot taketh hold, His way, have I kept, and not swerved;
text.KJV: My foot hath held his steps, his way have I kept, and not declined.
text.NABRE: My foot has always walked in his steps; I have kept his way and not turned aside.
text.DRB: My foot hath followed his steps, I have kept his way, & have not declined out of it.

text.NRSV: I have not departed from the commandment of his lips; I have treasured in my bosom the words of his mouth.
text.Rotherham: The command of his lips, and would not go back, and, in my bosom, have I treasured the words of his lips.
text.KJV: Neither have I gone back from the commandment of his lips; I have esteemed the words of his mouth more than my necessary food.
text.NABRE: From the commands of his lips I have not departed; the words of his mouth I have treasured in my heart.
text.DRB: From the commandments of his lips I have not departed, and I have hid the words of his mouth in my bosom.

text.NRSV: But he is unchangeable and who can turn him? What he desires, that he does.
text.Rotherham: But, he, is one, and who can turn him? What his soul desired, he hath done.
text.KJV: But he is in one mind, and who can turn him? and what his soul desireth, even that he doeth.
text.NABRE: But once he decides, who can contradict him? What he desires, that he does.
text.DRB: For he is alone, and no man can turn away his cogitation: and whatsoever his soul would, that hath he done.

text.NRSV: For he will complete what he appoints for me; and many such things are in his mind.
text.Rotherham: Surely he will accomplish what is decreed for me, and, many such things, hath he in store.
text.KJV: For he performeth the thing that is appointed for me: and many such things are with him.
text.NABRE: For he will carry out what is appointed for me, and many such things he has in store.
text.DRB: And when he shall have fulfilled his will in me, many other things also are at hand with him.

text.NRSV: Therefore I am terrified at his presence; when I consider, I am in dread of him.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, from his presence, am I driven in fear, I diligently consider and am kept back from him in dread:
text.KJV: Therefore am I troubled at his presence: when I consider, I am afraid of him.
text.NABRE: Therefore I am terrified before him; when I take thought, I dread him.
text.DRB: And therefore I am troubled at his face, and considering him I am made pensive with fear.

text.NRSV: God has made my heart faint; the Almighty has terrified me;
text.Rotherham: Yea, GOD, hath made timid my heart, and, the Almighty, hath put me in terror.
text.KJV: For God maketh my heart soft, and the Almighty troubleth me:
text.NABRE: For it is God who has made my heart faint, the Almighty who has terrified me.
text.DRB: God hath mollified my heart, and the Omnipotent hath troubled me.

text.NRSV: If only I could vanish in darkness, and thick darkness would cover my face!
text.Rotherham: Because I was not cut off before the darkness, nor, before my face, did the gloom form a shroud.
text.KJV: Because I was not cut off before the darkness, neither hath he covered the darkness from my face.
text.NABRE: Yes, would that I had vanished in darkness, hidden by the thick gloom before me.
text.DRB: For I have not perished because of the imminent darkness, neither hath the mist covered my face.

text.NRSV: “Why are times not kept by the Almighty, and why do those who know him never see his days?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, since from the Almighty times are not hid, have, his knowing ones, no vision of his days?
text.KJV: Why, seeing times are not hidden from the Almighty, do they that know him not see his days?
text.NABRE: Why are times not set by the Almighty, and why do his friends not see his days?
text.DRB: Times are not hid from the Omnipotent: but they that know him, know not his days.

text.NRSV: The wicked remove landmarks; they seize flocks and pasture them.
text.Rotherham: Boundaries, men move back, flocks, they seize and consume;
text.KJV: Some remove the landmarks; they violently take away flocks, and feed thereof.
text.NABRE: People remove landmarks; they steal herds and pasture them.
text.DRB: Some have transferred bounds, spoiled flocks, & fed them.

text.NRSV: They drive away the donkey of the orphan; they take the widow’s ox for a pledge.
text.Rotherham: The ass of the fatherless, they drive off, they take in pledge the ox of the widow;
text.KJV: They drive away the ass of the fatherless, they take the widow’s ox for a pledge.
text.NABRE: The donkeys of orphans they drive away; they take the widow’s ox for a pledge.
text.DRB: They have driven away the ass of pupils, and have taken away the widow's oxe for a pledge.

text.NRSV: They thrust the needy off the road; the poor of the earth all hide themselves.
text.Rotherham: They turn aside the needy out of the way, at once, are the humbled of the land made to hide themselves.
text.KJV: They turn the needy out of the way: the poor of the earth hide themselves together.
text.NABRE: They force the needy off the road; all the poor of the land are driven into hiding.
text.DRB: They have subverted the way of the poor, and have oppressed together the meek of the earth.

text.NRSV: Like wild donkeys in the desert they go out to their toil, scavenging for food; the wasteland provides food for their children.
text.Rotherham: Lo! [as] wild asses in the wilderness, they go forth with their work, eager seekers for prey, the waste plain, yieldeth them food for their young;
text.KJV: Behold, as wild asses in the desert, go they forth to their work; rising betimes for a prey: the wilderness yieldeth food for them and for their children.
text.NABRE: Like wild donkeys in the wilderness, they go forth to their task of seeking prey; the steppe provides food for their young;
text.DRB: Others as wild asses in the desert go forth to their work: watching to the prey, do prepare bread for their children.

text.NRSV: They reap in a field not their own and they glean in the vineyard of the wicked.
text.Rotherham: In the field—a man’s fodder, they cut down, and, the vineyard of the lawless, they strip of its late berries;
text.KJV: They reap every one his corn in the field: and they gather the vintage of the wicked.
text.NABRE: They harvest fodder in the field, and glean in the vineyard of the wicked.
text.DRB: They reap the field that is not theirs, and gather the grapes of his vineyard, whom by violence they have oppressed.

text.NRSV: They lie all night naked, without clothing, and have no covering in the cold.
text.Rotherham: Ill-clad, they are left to lodge without clothing, and have no covering in the cold;
text.KJV: They cause the naked to lodge without clothing, that they have no covering in the cold.
text.NABRE: They pass the night naked, without clothing; they have no covering against the cold;
text.DRB: They send men away naked taking away their cloths, which have no covering in the cold.

text.NRSV: They are wet with the rain of the mountains, and cling to the rock for want of shelter.
text.Rotherham: With the sweeping rain of the mountains, are they wet, and, through having no shelter, they embrace a rock.
text.KJV: They are wet with the showers of the mountains, and embrace the rock for want of a shelter.
text.NABRE: They are drenched with rain from the mountains, and for want of shelter they cling to the rock.
text.DRB: Whom the showers of the mountains do wash, & not having a covert, they embrace stones.

text.NRSV: “There are those who snatch the orphan child from the breast, and take as a pledge the infant of the poor.
text.Rotherham: Men tear, from the breast, the fatherless, and, over the poor, they take a pledge;
text.KJV: They pluck the fatherless from the breast, and take a pledge of the poor.
text.NABRE: Orphans are snatched from the breast, infants of the needy are taken in pledge.
text.DRB: They did violence spoiling the pupils, & the common poor people they spoiled.

text.NRSV: They go about naked, without clothing; hungry, they carry the sheaves;
text.Rotherham: Naked, they go about without clothing, and, famished, they carry the sheaves;
text.KJV: They cause him to go naked without clothing, and they take away the sheaf from the hungry;
text.NABRE: They go about naked, without clothing, and famished, they carry the sheaves.
text.DRB: From the naked & them that go without clothing, and the hungry they have taken away the ears of corn.

text.NRSV: between the terrace walls they press out oil; they tread the wine presses, but suffer thirst.
text.Rotherham: Between their walls, are they exposed to the sun, Wine-presses, they tread, and yet are thirsty;
text.KJV: Which make oil within their walls, and tread their winepresses, and suffer thirst.
text.NABRE: Between the rows they press out the oil; they tread the wine presses, yet are thirsty.
text.DRB: They have rested the noontide among their heaps, which having trodden the wine presses are a thirst.

text.NRSV: From the city the dying groan, and the throat of the wounded cries for help; yet God pays no attention to their prayer.
text.Rotherham: Out of the city—out of the houses, they make outcry. and, the soul of the wounded, calleth for help, and, GOD, doth not regard it as foolish.
text.KJV: Men groan from out of the city, and the soul of the wounded crieth out: yet God layeth not folly to them.
text.NABRE: In the city the dying groan, and the souls of the wounded cry out. Yet God does not treat it as a disgrace!
text.DRB: Out of the cities they have made men to mourn, and the soul of the wounded hath cried, & God doth not suffer it to pass unrevenged:

text.NRSV: “There are those who rebel against the light, who are not acquainted with its ways, and do not stay in its paths.
text.Rotherham: They, have become rebels against the light,—they are not acquainted with the ways thereof, neither abide they in the paths thereof.
text.KJV: They are of those that rebel against the light; they know not the ways thereof, nor abide in the paths thereof.
text.NABRE: They are rebels against the light: they do not recognize its ways; they do not stay in its paths.
text.DRB: They have been rebellious to the light, they have not known his ways, neither did they return by his paths.

text.NRSV: The murderer rises at twilight to kill the poor and needy, and in the night is like a thief.
text.Rotherham: With the light, riseth the murderer, He slayeth the poor and needy, And, in the night, he becometh like a thief.
text.KJV: The murderer rising with the light killeth the poor and needy, and in the night is as a thief.
text.NABRE: When there is no light the murderer rises, to kill the poor and needy; in the night he acts like a thief.
text.DRB: At the very break of day the murderer riseth, he killeth the needy, and the poor man: but by night he will be as a thief.

text.NRSV: The eye of the adulterer also waits for the twilight, saying, ‘No eye will see me’; and he disguises his face.
text.Rotherham: And, the eye of the adulterer, watcheth for the evening twilight, saying, Not an eye will see me! A covering for the face, he putteth on;
text.KJV: The eye also of the adulterer waiteth for the twilight, saying, No eye shall see me: and disguiseth his face.
text.NABRE: The eye of the adulterer watches for the twilight; he says, “No eye will see me.” He puts a mask over his face;
text.DRB: The eye of the adulterer observeth darkness, saying: Eye shall not see me: and he will cover his face.

text.NRSV: In the dark they dig through houses; by day they shut themselves up; they do not know the light.
text.Rotherham: He breaketh, in the dark, into houses,—By day, they lock themselves in, They know not the light;
text.KJV: In the dark they dig through houses, which they had marked for themselves in the daytime: they know not the light.
text.NABRE: in the dark he breaks into houses; By day they shut themselves in; they do not know the light.
text.DRB: He diggeth through houses in the dark, as in the day they had appointed with themselves, and they have not known the light.

text.NRSV: For deep darkness is morning to all of them; for they are friends with the terrors of deep darkness.
text.Rotherham: For, in the case of all such, morning to them is the death-shade, For, to be recognised, is a death-shade terror.
text.KJV: For the morning is to them even as the shadow of death: if one know them, they are in the terrors of the shadow of death.
text.NABRE: Indeed, for all of them morning is deep darkness; then they recognize the terrors of deep darkness.
text.DRB: If suddenly the morning shall appear, they think it the shadow of death: and they walk so in darkness as it were in light.

text.NRSV: “Swift are they on the face of the waters; their portion in the land is cursed; no treader turns toward their vineyards.
text.Rotherham: Swift is he on the face of the waters, Speedily vanished their share in the land, He turneth not to the way of the vineyards.
text.KJV: He is swift as the waters; their portion is cursed in the earth: he beholdeth not the way of the vineyards.
text.NABRE: He is swift on the surface of the water: their portion in the land is accursed, they do not turn aside by way of the vineyards.
text.DRB: He is light above the face of the water: cursed be his portion in the earth, neither walk he by the way of the vineyards.

text.NRSV: Drought and heat snatch away the snow waters; so does Sheol those who have sinned.
text.Rotherham: Drought and heat, steal away snow water, Hades, them who have sinned.
text.KJV: Drought and heat consume the snow waters: so doth the grave those which have sinned.
text.NABRE: Drought and heat snatch away the snow waters, Sheol, those who have sinned.
text.DRB: Let him pass from snow waters to exceeding heat, and his sin even unto hell.

text.NRSV: The womb forgets them; the worm feeds sweetly on them; they are no longer remembered; so wickedness is broken like a tree.
text.Rotherham: Maternal love shall forget him, the worm shall find him sweet, No more shall he be remembered, but perversity shall be shivered like a tree.
text.KJV: The womb shall forget him; the worm shall feed sweetly on him; he shall be no more remembered; and wickedness shall be broken as a tree.
text.NABRE: May the womb forget him, may the worm find him sweet, may he no longer be remembered; And may wickedness be broken like a tree.
text.DRB: Let mercy forget him: worms his sweetness: be he not in remembrance, but be he broken in pieces as an unfruitful tree.

text.NRSV: “They harm the childless woman, and do no good to the widow.
text.Rotherham: He oppresseth the barren who beareth not, and, to the widow, he doeth not good;
text.KJV: He evil entreateth the barren that beareth not: and doeth not good to the widow.
text.NABRE: May his companion be barren, unable to give birth, may his widow not prosper!
text.DRB: For he hath fed the barren, and her that beareth not, and to the widow he hath not done good.

text.NRSV: Yet God prolongs the life of the mighty by his power; they rise up when they despair of life.
text.Rotherham: Yea he draggeth along the mighty by his strength, He riseth up, and none hath assurance of life;
text.KJV: He draweth also the mighty with his power: he riseth up, and no man is sure of life.
text.NABRE: He sustains the mighty by his strength, to him who rises without assurance of his life
text.DRB: He hath pulled down the strong in his strength and when he shall stand, he will not credit his life.

text.NRSV: He gives them security, and they are supported; his eyes are upon their ways.
text.Rotherham: It is given him to be secure, and confident, yet, his eyes, are upon their ways.
text.KJV: Though it be given him to be in safety, whereon he resteth; yet his eyes are upon their ways.
text.NABRE: he gives safety and support, and his eyes are on their ways.
text.DRB: God hath given him place for penance, and he abuseth it unto pride: but his eyes be upon his ways.

text.NRSV: They are exalted for a little while, and then are gone; they wither and fade like the mallow; they are cut off like the heads of grain.
text.Rotherham: They are exalted a little, and are not, Yea having been laid low, like all men, are they gathered, Even as the top of an ear of corn, do they hang down.
text.KJV: They are exalted for a little while, but are gone and brought low; they are taken out of the way as all other, and cut off as the tops of the ears of corn.
text.NABRE: They are exalted for a while, and then are no more; laid low, like everyone else they are gathered up; like ears of grain they shrivel.
text.DRB: They are elevated for a little, and shall not stand, and shall be humbled as all things, and shall be taken away, and as the tops of the ears of corn they shall be broken.

text.NRSV: If it is not so, who will prove me a liar, and show that there is nothing in what I say?”
text.Rotherham: But, if not, who then can convict me of falsehood? or make of no account my words?
text.KJV: And if it be not so now, who will make me a liar, and make my speech nothing worth?
text.NABRE: If this be not so, who can make me a liar, and reduce my words to nothing?
text.DRB: And if it be not so, who can reprove me that I have lied, and set my words before God?

text.NRSV: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Bildad the Shuhite, and said:—
text.KJV: Then answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,
text.NABRE: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:
text.DRB: But Baldad the Suhite answering, said:

text.NRSV: “Dominion and fear are with God; he makes peace in his high heaven.
text.Rotherham: Dominion and dread, are with him, who causeth prosperity among his lofty ones;
text.KJV: Dominion and fear are with him, he maketh peace in his high places.
text.NABRE: Dominion and dread are his who brings about harmony in his heavens.
text.DRB: Power and terror is with him, that maketh concord in his high ones.

text.NRSV: Is there any number to his armies? Upon whom does his light not arise?
text.Rotherham: Is there any number to his troops? And upon whom ariseth not his light?
text.KJV: Is there any number of his armies? and upon whom doth not his light arise?
text.NABRE: Is there any numbering of his troops? Yet on which of them does his light not rise?
text.DRB: Is there any number of his soldiers? And upon whom shall not his light arise?

text.NRSV: How then can a mortal be righteous before God? How can one born of woman be pure?
text.Rotherham: How then shall, a mortal, be just with GOD? Or how shall he be pure who is born of a woman?
text.KJV: How then can man be justified with God? or how can he be clean that is born of a woman?
text.NABRE: How can anyone be in the right against God, or how can any born of woman be innocent?
text.DRB: Can man be justified compared with God, or the born of a woman appear clean?

text.NRSV: If even the moon is not bright and the stars are not pure in his sight,
text.Rotherham: Look as far as the moon, and it is not clear, and, the stars, are not bright in his eyes!
text.KJV: Behold even to the moon, and it shineth not; yea, the stars are not pure in his sight.
text.NABRE: Even the moon is not bright and the stars are not clean in his eyes.
text.DRB: Behold the moon also doth not shine, and the stars are not clean in his sight.

text.NRSV: how much less a mortal, who is a maggot, and a human being, who is a worm!”
text.Rotherham: How much less a mortal who is a creeping thing? Or a son of the earth-born who is a worm?
text.KJV: How much less man, that is a worm? and the son of man, which is a worm?
text.NABRE: How much less a human being, who is but a worm, a mortal, who is only a maggot?
text.DRB: How much more man rottenness, and the son of man a worm?

text.NRSV: Then Job answered:
text.Rotherham: Then responded Job, and said:—
text.KJV: But Job answered and said,
text.NABRE: Then Job answered and said:
text.DRB: But Job answering, said:

text.NRSV: “How you have helped one who has no power! How you have assisted the arm that has no strength!
text.Rotherham: How hast thou given help to one of no-strength? given victory to an arm of no-power?
text.KJV: How hast thou helped him that is without power? how savest thou the arm that hath no strength?
text.NABRE: What help you give to the powerless, what strength to the feeble arm!
text.DRB: Whose helper are thou? His that is weak? And doest thou hold up the arm of him, that is not strong?

text.NRSV: How you have counseled one who has no wisdom, and given much good advice!
text.Rotherham: How hast thou given counsel to one of no-wisdom? or, effective wisdom, abundantly made known?
text.KJV: How hast thou counselled him that hath no wisdom? and how hast thou plentifully declared the thing as it is?
text.NABRE: How you give counsel to one without wisdom; how profuse is the advice you offer!
text.DRB: To whom hast thou given counsel? Perhaps to him, that hath not wisdom, and thy prudence hast thou shewed very great.

text.NRSV: With whose help have you uttered words, and whose spirit has come forth from you?
text.Rotherham: Whom hast thou taught speech? Whose inspiration hath come from thee?
text.KJV: To whom hast thou uttered words? and whose spirit came from thee?
text.NABRE: With whose help have you uttered those words, whose breath comes forth from you?
text.DRB: Whom wouldest thou teach? Not him that made breath?

text.NRSV: The shades below tremble, the waters and their inhabitants.
text.Rotherham: The shades, tremble, beneath the waters and their inhabitants;
text.KJV: Dead things are formed from under the waters, and the inhabitants thereof.
text.NABRE: The shades beneath writhe in terror, the waters, and their inhabitants.
text.DRB: Behold the giants groan under the waters, & they that dwell with them.

text.NRSV: Sheol is naked before God, and Abaddon has no covering.
text.Rotherham: Naked is hades before him, and there is no covering to destruction;
text.KJV: Hell is naked before him, and destruction hath no covering.
text.NABRE: Naked before him is Sheol, and Abaddon has no covering.
text.DRB: Hell is naked before him, & there is no covert to perdition.

text.NRSV: He stretches out the north over the void, and hangs the earth upon nothing.
text.Rotherham: Who stretcheth out the north over emptiness, hangeth the earth upon nothingness;
text.KJV: He stretcheth out the north over the empty place, and hangeth the earth upon nothing.
text.NABRE: He stretches out Zaphon over the void, and suspends the earth over nothing at all;
text.DRB: Who stretcheth out the north-wind over the vacant, and hangeth the earth upon nothing.

text.NRSV: He binds up the waters in his thick clouds, and the cloud is not torn under them.
text.Rotherham: Who bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds, and the cloud is not rent beneath them;
text.KJV: He bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds; and the cloud is not rent under them.
text.NABRE: He binds up the waters in his clouds, yet the cloud is not split by their weight;
text.DRB: Who bindeth the waters in his clouds, that they break not forth together downward.

text.NRSV: He covers the face of the full moon, and spreads over it his cloud.
text.Rotherham: Who shutteth-in the face of the throne, he spreadeth over it his cloud;
text.KJV: He holdeth back the face of his throne, and spreadeth his cloud upon it.
text.NABRE: He holds back the appearance of the full moon by spreading his clouds before it.
text.DRB: Who holdeth the countenance of his throne, & spreadeth his cloud over it.

text.NRSV: He has described a circle on the face of the waters, at the boundary between light and darkness.
text.Rotherham: A, boundary, hath he encircled on the face of the waters, as far as where light ends in darkness;
text.KJV: He hath compassed the waters with bounds, until the day and night come to an end.
text.NABRE: He has marked out a circle on the surface of the deep as the boundary of light and darkness.
text.DRB: He hath made a limit about the waters, till light and darkness be ended.

text.NRSV: The pillars of heaven tremble, and are astounded at his rebuke.
text.Rotherham: The pillars of the heavens, are shaken, and are terrified at his rebuke:
text.KJV: The pillars of heaven tremble and are astonished at his reproof.
text.NABRE: The pillars of the heavens tremble and are stunned at his thunderous rebuke;
text.DRB: The pillars of heaven tremble, and dread at his beck.

text.NRSV: By his power he stilled the Sea; by his understanding he struck down Rahab.
text.Rotherham: By his strength, hath he excited the sea, and, by his skill, hath he shattered the Crocodile:
text.KJV: He divideth the sea with his power, and by his understanding he smiteth through the proud.
text.NABRE: By his power he stilled Sea, by his skill he crushed Rahab;
text.DRB: In his strength suddenly the seas are gathered together, and with his wisdom he struck the proud man.

text.NRSV: By his wind the heavens were made fair; his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
text.Rotherham: By his spirit, hath he arched the heavens, His hand hath pierced the fleeing serpent.
text.KJV: By his spirit he hath garnished the heavens; his hand hath formed the crooked serpent.
text.NABRE: By his wind the heavens were made clear, his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
text.DRB: His spirit hath adorned the heavens & his hand being the mid-wife, the winding serpent is brought forth.

text.NRSV: These are indeed but the outskirts of his ways; and how small a whisper do we hear of him! But the thunder of his power who can understand?”
text.Rotherham: Lo! these, are the fringes of his way, and what a whisper of a word hath been heard of him! But, the thunder of his might, who could understand?
text.KJV: Lo, these are parts of his ways: but how little a portion is heard of him? but the thunder of his power who can understand?
text.NABRE: Lo, these are but the outlines of his ways, and what a whisper of a word we hear of him: Who can comprehend the thunder of his power?
text.DRB: Lo, these things are said in part of his ways: and where as we have heard scarce a little drop of his word, who shall be able to behold the thunder of his greatness?

text.NRSV: Job again took up his discourse and said:
text.Rotherham: And Job again took up his measure, and said:—
text.KJV: Moreover Job continued his parable, and said,
text.NABRE: Job took up his theme again and said:
text.DRB: Job also added, taking his parable, and said:

text.NRSV: “As God lives, who has taken away my right, and the Almighty, who has made my soul bitter,
text.Rotherham: As GOD liveth who hath taken away my right, even the Almighty, who hath embittered my soul;
text.KJV: As God liveth, who hath taken away my judgment; and the Almighty, who hath vexed my soul;
text.NABRE: As God lives, who takes away my right, the Almighty, who has made my life bitter,
text.DRB: God liveth, who hath taken away my judgment, and the Omnipotent, which hath brought my soul to bitterness.

text.NRSV: as long as my breath is in me and the spirit of God is in my nostrils,
text.Rotherham: All the while my inspiration is in me, and the spirit of GOD is in my nostrils,
text.KJV: All the while my breath is in me, and the spirit of God is in my nostrils;
text.NABRE: So long as I still have life breath in me, the breath of God in my nostrils,
text.DRB: That as long as breath remaineth in me, and the spirit of God in my nostrils,

text.NRSV: my lips will not speak falsehood, and my tongue will not utter deceit.
text.Rotherham: Verily my lips shall not speak perversity, nor shall, my tongue, utter deceit.
text.KJV: My lips shall not speak wickedness, nor my tongue utter deceit.
text.NABRE: My lips shall not speak falsehood, nor my tongue utter deceit!
text.DRB: My lips shall not speak iniquity, neither shall my tongue meditate lying.

text.NRSV: Far be it from me to say that you are right; until I die I will not put away my integrity from me.
text.Rotherham: Far be it from me! that I should justify, you,—Even until I breathe my last, will I not let go mine integrity from me:
text.KJV: God forbid that I should justify you: till I die I will not remove mine integrity from me.
text.NABRE: Far be it from me to account you right; till I die I will not renounce my innocence.
text.DRB: God forbid that I should judge you to be just: till I fail, I will not depart from mine innocency.

text.NRSV: I hold fast my righteousness, and will not let it go; my heart does not reproach me for any of my days.
text.Rotherham: On my righteousness, have I taken fast hold, and will not give it up, My heart shall not reproach any of my days.
text.KJV: My righteousness I hold fast, and will not let it go: my heart shall not reproach me so long as I live.
text.NABRE: My justice I maintain and I will not relinquish it; my heart does not reproach me for any of my days.
text.DRB: My justification which I have begun to hold, I will not forsake: for my heart doth not reprehend me in all my life.

text.NRSV: “May my enemy be like the wicked, and may my opponent be like the unrighteous.
text.Rotherham: Let mine enemy be a veritably lawless one! And, he that lifteth himself up against me, one veritably perverse!
text.KJV: Let mine enemy be as the wicked, and he that riseth up against me as the unrighteous.
text.NABRE: Let my enemy be as the wicked and my adversary as the unjust!
text.DRB: Let mine enemy be as the impious, and mine adversary as the wicked one.

text.NRSV: For what is the hope of the godless when God cuts them off, when God takes away their lives?
text.Rotherham: For what shall be the hope of the impious, though he graspeth with greed, when GOD shall draw forth his soul?
text.KJV: For what is the hope of the hypocrite, though he hath gained, when God taketh away his soul?
text.NABRE: For what hope has the impious when he is cut off, when God requires his life?
text.DRB: For what is the hypocrite's hope, if covetously he take by violence, and God deliver not his soul?

text.NRSV: Will God hear their cry when trouble comes upon them?
text.Rotherham: His outcry, will GOD hear, when there cometh upon him distress?
text.KJV: Will God hear his cry when trouble cometh upon him?
text.NABRE: Will God then listen to his cry when distress comes upon him,
text.DRB: Will God hear his cry, when distress shall come upon him?

text.NRSV: Will they take delight in the Almighty? Will they call upon God at all times?
text.Rotherham: Verily, in the Almighty, he will not find delight, nor call on GOD continually!
text.KJV: Will he delight himself in the Almighty? will he always call upon God?
text.NABRE: If he delights in the Almighty and calls upon God constantly?
text.DRB: Or can he be delighted in the Omnipotent, and invocate God at all time?

text.NRSV: I will teach you about the hand of God; that which is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
text.Rotherham: I would teach you, by the hand of GOD, That which is with the Almighty, will I not conceal.
text.KJV: I will teach you by the hand of God: that which is with the Almighty will I not conceal.
text.NABRE: I will teach you what is in God’s hand, and the way of the Almighty I will not conceal.
text.DRB: I will teach you by the hand of God, what the Omnipotent hath, neither will I hide it.

text.NRSV: See, all of you have seen it yourselves; why then have you become altogether vain?
text.Rotherham: Lo! ye, have, all of you, seen, Wherefore, then, is it, that ye are utterly without purpose?
text.KJV: Behold, all ye yourselves have seen it; why then are ye thus altogether vain?
text.NABRE: Look, you yourselves have all seen it; why do you spend yourselves in empty words!
text.DRB: Lo, you do all know, and why speak you vain things without cause?

text.NRSV: “This is the portion of the wicked with God, and the heritage that oppressors receive from the Almighty:
text.Rotherham: This, is the portion of a lawless man with GOD, That, the heritage of tyrants—from the Almighty, he shall receive.
text.KJV: This is the portion of a wicked man with God, and the heritage of oppressors, which they shall receive of the Almighty.
text.NABRE: This is the portion of the wicked with God, the heritage oppressors receive from the Almighty:
text.DRB: This is the portion of an impious man with God, and inheritance of the violent, which they shall receive of the Omnipotent.

text.NRSV: If their children are multiplied, it is for the sword; and their offspring have not enough to eat.
text.Rotherham: If his children be multiplied, for them, [there is] the sword, and, his offspring, shall not be filled with bread;
text.KJV: If his children be multiplied, it is for the sword: and his offspring shall not be satisfied with bread.
text.NABRE: Though his children be many, the sword awaits them. His descendants shall want for bread.
text.DRB: If his children be multiplied, they shall be in the sword, & his nephews shall not be filled with bread.

text.NRSV: Those who survive them are buried by pestilence, and their widows make no lamentation.
text.Rotherham: His survivors, by pestilence, shall come to the grave, and, his widows, shall not weep;
text.KJV: Those that remain of him shall be buried in death: and his widows shall not weep.
text.NABRE: His survivors shall be buried in death; their widows shall not weep.
text.DRB: They that shall be left of him, shall be buried in death, and his widows shall not weep.

text.NRSV: Though they heap up silver like dust, and pile up clothing like clay—
text.Rotherham: Though he heap up silver like, dust, and, like a pile, he prepare clothing,
text.KJV: Though he heap up silver as the dust, and prepare raiment as the clay;
text.NABRE: Though he heap up silver like dust and store away mounds of clothing,
text.DRB: If he shall heap together silver as earth, and as clay shall prepare garments:

text.NRSV: they may pile it up, but the just will wear it, and the innocent will divide the silver.
text.Rotherham: He may prepare, but, the righteous, shall put on, and, the silver, shall the innocent apportion.
text.KJV: He may prepare it, but the just shall put it on, and the innocent shall divide the silver.
text.NABRE: What he has stored the righteous shall wear, and the innocent shall divide the silver.
text.DRB: He shall prepare indeed, but the just man shall be clothed with them: and the innocent shall divide the silver.

text.NRSV: They build their houses like a moth’s, like a booth that a watchman makes.
text.Rotherham: He hath built, like a moth, his house,—like a hut, which a watcher hath made.
text.KJV: He buildeth his house as a moth, and as a booth that the keeper maketh.
text.NABRE: He builds his house as of cobwebs, or like a booth put up by a watchman.
text.DRB: He hath built his house as a moth, and as a keeper hath he made a bower.

text.NRSV: They go to bed wealth, but will do so no more; they open their eyes, and it is gone.
text.Rotherham: The rich man, shall lie down, and not do it again, his eyes, hath he opened, and then is not.
text.KJV: The rich man shall lie down, but he shall not be gathered: he openeth his eyes, and he is not.
text.NABRE: He lies down a rich man, one last time; he opens his eyes—nothing is there.
text.DRB: The rich man when he shall sleep shall take away nothing with him: he shall open his eyes, and find nothing.

text.NRSV: Terrors overtake them like a flood; in the night a tempest carries them off.
text.Rotherham: There shall reach him—like waters—terrors, By night, a storm-wind hath stolen him away;
text.KJV: Terrors take hold on him as waters, a tempest stealeth him away in the night.
text.NABRE: Terrors flood over him like water, at night the tempest carries him off.
text.DRB: Poverty as water shall apprehend him, in the night a tempest shall oppress him.

text.NRSV: The east wind lifts them up and they are gone; it sweeps them out of their place.
text.Rotherham: An east wind shall lift him up, and he shall depart, and it shall sweep him away out of his place;
text.KJV: The east wind carrieth him away, and he departeth: and as a storm hurleth him out of his place.
text.NABRE: The east wind seizes him and he is gone; it sweeps him from his place;
text.DRB: The burning wind shall take him up, and carry him away, and as a whirlwind shall pull him violently out of his place.

text.NRSV: It hurls at them without pity; they flee from its power in headlong flight.
text.Rotherham: And He will cast upon him and not spare, Out of his hand, shall he, swiftly flee;
text.KJV: For God shall cast upon him, and not spare: he would fain flee out of his hand.
text.NABRE: It hurls itself at him without pity, as he tries to flee from its power.
text.DRB: And he shall cast upon him, and shall not spare: out of his hand flying he shall fly.

text.NRSV: It claps its hands at them, and hisses at them from its place.
text.Rotherham: He shall clap over him his hands, and shall hiss him forth out of his place.
text.KJV: Men shall clap their hands at him, and shall hiss him out of his place.
text.NABRE: It claps its hands at him, and whistles at him from its place.
text.DRB: He shall clasp his hands upon him, and shall hiss upon him, beholding his place.

text.NRSV: “Surely there is a mine for silver, and a place for gold to be refined.
text.Rotherham: Though there is, for silver, a vein, and a place for the gold they refine;
text.KJV: Surely there is a vein for the silver, and a place for gold where they fine it.
text.NABRE: V. The Poem on Wisdom Chapter 28 Where Is Wisdom to Be Found? There is indeed a mine for silver, and a place for refining gold.
text.DRB: Silver hath beginnings of her veins, and gold hath a place, wherein it is molten.

text.NRSV: Iron is taken out of the earth, and copper is smelted from ore.
text.Rotherham: Iron, out of the ore, is taken, and, stone, poureth out copper;
text.KJV: Iron is taken out of the earth, and brass is molten out of the stone.
text.NABRE: Iron is taken from the earth, and copper smelted out of stone.
text.DRB: Iron is taken out of the earth, & stone resolved with heat, is turned into brass.

text.NRSV: Miners put an end to darkness, and search out to the farthest bound the ore in gloom and deep darkness.
text.Rotherham: An end, hath one set to the darkness, and, into every extremity, is, he, making search, for the stone of darkness and death-shade;
