text.DRB: If there shall be an Angel speaking for him, one of thousands, to declare man's equity.

text.NRSV: and he is gracious to that person, and says, ‘Deliver him from going down into the Pit; I have found a ransom;
text.Rotherham: Then hath he shewed him favour, and said, Set him free from going down to the pit, I have found a price of redemption!
text.KJV: Then he is gracious unto him, and saith, Deliver him from going down to the pit: I have found a ransom.
text.NABRE: He will take pity on him and say, “Deliver him from going down to the pit; I have found him a ransom.”
text.DRB: He shall have mercy on him, and shall say: deliver him, that he descend not into corruption: I have found wherein I may be propitious to him.

text.NRSV: let his flesh become fresh with youth; let him return to the days of his youthful vigor’;
text.Rotherham: His flesh hath been made fresher than a child’s, he hath returned to the days of his youth;
text.KJV: His flesh shall be fresher than a child’s: he shall return to the days of his youth:
text.NABRE: Then his flesh shall become soft as a boy’s; he shall be again as in the days of his youth.
text.DRB: His flesh is consumed with punishments, let it return to the days of his youth.

text.NRSV: then he prays to God, and is accepted by him, he comes into his presence with joy, and God repays him for his righteousness.
text.Rotherham: He made supplication unto GOD, who hath accepted him, and he hath beheld his face with a shout of triumph, Thus hath he given back to man his righteousness.
text.KJV: He shall pray unto God, and he will be favourable unto him: and he shall see his face with joy: for he will render unto man his righteousness.
text.NABRE: He shall pray and God will favor him; he shall see God’s face with rejoicing; for he restores a person’s righteousness.
text.DRB: He shall beseech God, and he will be pacified towards him: and he shall see his face in jubilation, and he will render to a man his justice.

text.NRSV: That person sings to others and says, ‘I sinned, and perverted what was right, and it was not paid back to me.
text.Rotherham: He sang before men, and said, I sinned, and, uprightness, I perverted, yet he requited me not;
text.KJV: He looketh upon men, and if any say, I have sinned, and perverted that which was right, and it profited me not;
text.NABRE: He shall sing before all and say, “I sinned and did wrong, yet I was not punished accordingly.
text.DRB: He shall behold men, and shall say: I have sinned, and indeed I have offended, and as I was worthy, I have not received.

text.NRSV: He has redeemed my soul from going down into the Pit, and my life shall see the light.’
text.Rotherham: He hath ransomed my soul from passing away into the pit,—and, my life, in the light, shall have vision.
text.KJV: He will deliver his soul from going into the pit, and his life shall see the light.
text.NABRE: He delivered me from passing to the pit, and my life sees light.”
text.DRB: He hath delivered his soul that it should not go into death, but living should see the light.

text.NRSV: “God does all these things, twice, three times, with mortals,
text.Rotherham: Lo! a these things, doth GOD work, two ways, three, with a man;
text.KJV: Lo, all these things worketh God oftentimes with man,
text.NABRE: See, all these things God does, two, even three times, for a man,
text.DRB: Behold all these things doth God work three times in every one.

text.NRSV: to bring back their souls from the Pit, so that they may see the light of life.
text.Rotherham: To bring back his soul from the pit, to enlighten with the light of the living.
text.KJV: To bring back his soul from the pit, to be enlightened with the light of the living.
text.NABRE: Bringing back his soul from the pit to the light, in the light of the living.
text.DRB: That he may reclaim their souls from corruption, and illuminate them with the light of the living.

text.NRSV: Pay heed, O Job, listen to me; be silent, and I will speak.
text.Rotherham: Mark well, O Job, and hearken to me, Be silent, and, I, will speak:
text.KJV: Mark well, O Job, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I will speak.
text.NABRE: Be attentive, Job, listen to me! Be silent and I will speak.
text.DRB: Attend Job, and hear me: and hold thy peace, whilst I speak.

text.NRSV: If you have anything to say, answer me; speak, for I desire to justify you.
text.Rotherham: If there is anything to say, reply to me, Speak, for I desire to justify thee;
text.KJV: If thou hast any thing to say, answer me: speak, for I desire to justify thee.
text.NABRE: If you have anything to say, then answer me. Speak out! I should like to see you justified.
text.DRB: But if thou hast what to speak, answer me, speak, for I would thee to appear just.

text.NRSV: If not, listen to me; be silent, and I will teach you wisdom.”
text.Rotherham: If not, do, thou, hearken unto me, Be silent, that I may teach thee wisdom.
text.KJV: If not, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I shall teach thee wisdom.
text.NABRE: If not, then you listen to me; be silent, and I will teach you wisdom.
text.DRB: And if thou have not, hear me: hold thy peace, & I will teach thee wisdom.

text.NRSV: Then Elihu continued and said:
text.Rotherham: Furthermore Elihu responded, and said:—
text.KJV: Furthermore Elihu answered and said,
text.NABRE: Then Elihu answered and said:
text.DRB: Eliu therefore pronouncing spake these words also:

text.NRSV: “Hear my words, you wise men, and give ear to me, you who know;
text.Rotherham: Hear, ye wise men, my words, and, ye who know, give ear unto me;
text.KJV: Hear my words, O ye wise men; and give ear unto me, ye that have knowledge.
text.NABRE: Hear my discourse, you that are wise; you that have knowledge, listen to me!
text.DRB: Hear ye wise men my words: and ye learned harken to me:

text.NRSV: for the ear tests words as the palate tastes food.
text.Rotherham: For, the ear, trieth words, as, the palate, tasteth in eating.
text.KJV: For the ear trieth words, as the mouth tasteth meat.
text.NABRE: For the ear tests words, as the palate tastes food.
text.DRB: For the ear proveth words, and the throat discerneth meats by the taste.

text.NRSV: Let us choose what is right; let us determine among ourselves what is good.
text.Rotherham: What is right, let us choose for ourselves, Let us know, among ourselves, what is good;
text.KJV: Let us choose to us judgment: let us know among ourselves what is good.
text.NABRE: Let us choose what is right; let us determine among ourselves what is good.
text.DRB: Let us choose us judgment, and among us let us see what is the better.

text.NRSV: For Job has said, ‘I am innocent, and God has taken away my right;
text.Rotherham: For Job hath said—I am righteous, But, GOD, hath turned away my right;
text.KJV: For Job hath said, I am righteous: and God hath taken away my judgment.
text.NABRE: For Job has said, “I am innocent, but God has taken away what is my right.
text.DRB: Because Job said: I am just, and God hath subverted my judgment.

text.NRSV: in spite of my right I am counted a liar; my wound is incurable, though I am without transgression.’
text.Rotherham: Concerning mine own right, shall I tell a falsehood? Incurable is my disease—not for any transgression.
text.KJV: Should I lie against my right? my wound is incurable without transgression.
text.NABRE: I declare the judgment on me to be a lie; my arrow-wound is incurable, sinless though I am.”
text.DRB: For in judging me there is a lie: mine arrow is violent without any sin.

text.NRSV: Who is there like Job, who drinks up scoffing like water,
text.Rotherham: What man is like Job? He drinketh in scoffing like water;
text.KJV: What man is like Job, who drinketh up scorning like water?
text.NABRE: What man is like Job? He drinks in blasphemies like water,
text.DRB: What man is there as is Job, that drinketh scorning as it were water?

text.NRSV: who keeps company with evildoers and walks with the wicked?
text.Rotherham: And is on the way to keep company, with the workers of iniquity, and to walk with lawless men.
text.KJV: Which goeth in company with the workers of iniquity, and walketh with wicked men.
text.NABRE: Keeps company with evildoers and goes along with the wicked,
text.DRB: What goeth with them that work iniquity, and walketh with impious men?

text.NRSV: For he has said, ‘It profits one nothing to take delight in God.’
text.Rotherham: For he hath said, It profiteth not a man, when, his good pleasure, is with God.
text.KJV: For he hath said, It profiteth a man nothing that he should delight himself with God.
text.NABRE: When he says, “There is no profit in pleasing God.”
text.DRB: For he hath said: Man shall not please God although he run with him.

text.NRSV: “Therefore, hear me, you who have sense, far be it from God that he should do wickedness, and from the Almighty that he should do wrong.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, ye men of mind, hearken unto me,—Far be it, that, GOD, should be lawless, or, the Almighty, be perverse!
text.KJV: Therefore hearken unto me, ye men of understanding: far be it from God, that he should do wickedness; and from the Almighty, that he should commit iniquity.
text.NABRE: Therefore, you that have understanding, hear me: far be it from God to do wickedness; far from the Almighty to do wrong!
text.DRB: Therefore ye discreet men hear me, far from God be impiety, and iniquity from the Omnipotent.

text.NRSV: For according to their deeds he will repay them, and according to their ways he will make it befall them.
text.Rotherham: For, what any son of earth doeth, he repayeth him, and, according to every man’s course, he causeth him to find.
text.KJV: For the work of a man shall he render unto him, and cause every man to find according to his ways.
text.NABRE: Rather, he requites mortals for their conduct, and brings home to them their way of life.
text.DRB: For he will render a man his work, & according to the ways of every one he will recompense them.

text.NRSV: Of a truth, God will not do wickedly, and the Almighty will not pervert justice.
text.Rotherham: Nay, verily, GOD, will not condemn unjustly,—nor, the Almighty, pervert justice.
text.KJV: Yea, surely God will not do wickedly, neither will the Almighty pervert judgment.
text.NABRE: Surely, God cannot act wickedly, the Almighty cannot pervert justice.
text.DRB: For in very deed God will not condemn without cause, neither will the Omnipotent subvert judgment.

text.NRSV: Who gave him charge over the earth? Or who laid on him the whole world?
text.Rotherham: Who set him in charge of the earth? Or who appointed [him] the whole world?
text.KJV: Who hath given him a charge over the earth? or who hath disposed the whole world?
text.NABRE: Who gave him charge over the earth, or who set all the world in its place?
text.DRB: What other hath he appointed over the earth? or whom hath he set over the world, which he made?

text.NRSV: If he should take back his spirit to himself, and gather to himself his breath,
text.Rotherham: If he should set against him his heart, His spirit and his inspiration, unto himself he should withdraw.
text.KJV: If he set his heart upon man, if he gather unto himself his spirit and his breath;
text.NABRE: If he were to set his mind to it, gather to himself his spirit and breath,
text.DRB: If he direct his heart to him, he shall draw his spirit and breath unto him.

text.NRSV: all flesh would perish together, and all mortals return to dust.
text.Rotherham: All flesh together, would cease to breathe, and, the earth-born, unto dust, would return.
text.KJV: All flesh shall perish together, and man shall turn again unto dust.
text.NABRE: All flesh would perish together, and mortals return to dust.
text.DRB: All flesh shall fail together, and man shall return into ashes.

text.NRSV: “If you have understanding, hear this; listen to what I say.
text.Rotherham: If then [thou hast] understanding, hear this, Give thou ear to the teaching of my words:—
text.KJV: If now thou hast understanding, hear this: hearken to the voice of my words.
text.NABRE: Now you —understand, hear this! Listen to the words I speak!
text.DRB: If then thou have understanding, hear that is said, and harken to the voice of my speech.

text.NRSV: Shall one who hates justice govern? Will you condemn him who is righteous and mighty,
text.Rotherham: Shall, the very hater of right, control? Or, the just—the mighty one, wilt thou condemn?
text.KJV: Shall even he that hateth right govern? and wilt thou condemn him that is most just?
text.NABRE: Can an enemy of justice be in control, will you condemn the supreme Just One,
text.DRB: Can he that loveth not judgment, be healed? And how doest thou so far condemn him that is just?

text.NRSV: who says to a king, ‘You scoundrel!’ and to princes, ‘You wicked men!’;
text.Rotherham: Doth one say to a king, Abandoned one! Or, Lawless one! unto nobles?
text.KJV: Is it fit to say to a king, Thou art wicked? and to princes, Ye are ungodly?
text.NABRE: Who says to a king, “You are worthless!” and to nobles, “You are wicked!”
text.DRB: That saith to the king, Apostata: that calleth dukes impious:

text.NRSV: who shows no partiality to nobles, nor regards the rich more than the poor, for they are all the work of his hands?
text.Rotherham: For he hath shewn no respect of persons unto princes, neither hath he recognised the rich rather than the poor? For, the work of his hands, are they all.
text.KJV: How much less to him that accepteth not the persons of princes, nor regardeth the rich more than the poor? for they all are the work of his hands.
text.NABRE: Who neither favors the person of princes, nor respects the rich more than the poor? For they are all the work of his hands;
text.DRB: Who accepteth not the persons of Princes: nor hath known the tyrant, when he contended against the poor man: for all are the work of his hands.

text.NRSV: In a moment they die; at midnight the people are shaken and pass away, and the mighty are taken away by no human hand.
text.Rotherham: In a moment, they die, even in the middle of the night,—A people are convulsed when they pass away, A mighty one is removed, without hand;
text.KJV: In a moment shall they die, and the people shall be troubled at midnight, and pass away: and the mighty shall be taken away without hand.
text.NABRE: in a moment they die, even at midnight. People are shaken, and pass away, the powerful are removed without lifting a hand;
text.DRB: They shall suddenly die, and at mid-night peoples shall be troubled, & shall pass, & take away the violent without hand.

text.NRSV: “For his eyes are upon the ways of mortals, and he sees all their steps.
text.Rotherham: For, his eyes, are on the ways of a man, and, all his footsteps, he beholdeth,—
text.KJV: For his eyes are upon the ways of man, and he seeth all his goings.
text.NABRE: For his eyes are upon our ways, and all our steps he sees.
text.DRB: For his eyes are upon the ways of men, and he considereth all their steps.

text.NRSV: There is no gloom or deep darkness where evildoers may hide themselves.
text.Rotherham: No darkness, and no death-shade, where the workers of iniquity may hide.
text.KJV: There is no darkness, nor shadow of death, where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves.
text.NABRE: There is no darkness so dense that evildoers can hide in it.
text.DRB: There is not darkness, & there is not shadow of death, that they may be hid there which work iniquity.

text.NRSV: For he has not appointed a time for anyone to go before God in judgment.
text.Rotherham: For, unto no man, doth he appoint a repetition,—in going unto GOD in judgment;
text.KJV: For he will not lay upon man more than right; that he should enter into judgment with God.
text.NABRE: For no one has God set a time to come before him in judgment.
text.DRB: For it is no more in man's power to come to God into judgment.

text.NRSV: He shatters the mighty without investigation, and sets others in their place.
text.Rotherham: He shattereth mighty ones unsearchably, and setteth up others in their stead:
text.KJV: He shall break in pieces mighty men without number, and set others in their stead.
text.NABRE: Without inquiry he shatters the mighty, and appoints others in their place,
text.DRB: He shall destroy many, & innumerable, & shall make other to stand for them.

text.NRSV: Thus, knowing their works, he overturns them in the night, and they are crushed.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, he observeth their works,—and overturneth [them] in a night, and they are crushed;
text.KJV: Therefore he knoweth their works, and he overturneth them in the night, so that they are destroyed.
text.NABRE: Thus he discerns their works; overnight they are crushed.
text.DRB: For he knoweth their works: and therefore he shall bring night, and they shall be destroyed.

text.NRSV: He strikes them for their wickedness while others look on,
text.Rotherham: In the place of lawless men, hath he chastised them, in presence of beholders.
text.KJV: He striketh them as wicked men in the open sight of others;
text.NABRE: Where the wicked are, he strikes them, in a place where all can see,
text.DRB: As impious men he hath strucken them in the place of them that see.

text.NRSV: because they turned aside from following him, and had no regard for any of his ways,
text.Rotherham: Forasmuch, as they turned from following him, and, none of his ways, did they teach;
text.KJV: Because they turned back from him, and would not consider any of his ways:
text.NABRE: Because they turned away from him and did not understand his ways at all:
text.DRB: Who as it were of purpose have revolted from him, & would not understand all his ways:

text.NRSV: so that they caused the cry of the poor to come to him, and he heard the cry of the afflicted.
text.Rotherham: Causing to reach him the outcry of the poor, Yea, the outcry of the oppressed, he heareth.
text.KJV: So that they cause the cry of the poor to come unto him, and he heareth the cry of the afflicted.
text.NABRE: And made the cry of the poor reach him, so that he heard the cry of the afflicted.
text.DRB: That they might cause the cry of the needy man to come to him, & he hear the voice of the poor.

text.NRSV: When he is quiet, who can condemn? When he hides his face, who can behold him, whether it be a nation or a person?—
text.Rotherham: When, he, giveth quiet, who then shall condemn? And, when he hideth [his] face, who then shall sing of him? whether unto a nation or unto mankind altogether,
text.KJV: When he giveth quietness, who then can make trouble? and when he hideth his face, who then can behold him? whether it be done against a nation, or against a man only:
text.NABRE: If he is silent, who then can condemn? If he hides his face, who then can behold him, whether nation or individual?
text.DRB: For he granting peace, who is there that can condemn? After he shall hide his countenance, who is there that may behold him, both upon nations, and upon all men?

text.NRSV: so that the godless should not reign, or those who ensnare the people.
text.Rotherham: That impious men may not reign, nor be ensnarers of the people.
text.KJV: That the hypocrite reign not, lest the people be ensnared.
text.NABRE: Let an impious man not rule, nor those who ensnare their people.
text.DRB: Who maketh a man that is an hypocrite reign for the sins of the people.

text.NRSV: “For has anyone said to God, ‘I have endured punishment; I will offend no more;
text.Rotherham: For, unto GOD, hath one [ever] said—I have borne punishment, I will not be perverse;
text.KJV: Surely it is meet to be said unto God, I have borne chastisement, I will not offend any more:
text.NABRE: Should anyone say to God, “I accept my punishment; I will offend no more;
text.DRB: Therefore because I have spoken to God, thee also I will not prohibit.

text.NRSV: teach me what I do not see; if I have done iniquity, I will do it no more’?
text.Rotherham: What I see not, do, thou, shew me, If, perverseness, I have wrought, I will do it no more?
text.KJV: That which I see not teach thou me: if I have done iniquity, I will do no more.
text.NABRE: What I cannot see, teach me: if I have done wrong, I will do so no more,”
text.DRB: If I have erred, teach thou me: If I have spoken iniquity, I will add no more.

text.NRSV: Will he then pay back to suit you, because you reject it? For you must choose, and not I; therefore declare what you know.
text.Rotherham: According to thy mind, must he requite it, that thou hast refused? For, thou, must choose, and not, I, What then thou knowest, speak!
text.KJV: Should it be according to thy mind? he will recompense it, whether thou refuse, or whether thou choose; and not I: therefore speak what thou knowest.
text.NABRE: Would you then say that God must punish, when you are disdainful? It is you who must choose, not I; speak, therefore, what you know.
text.DRB: Doth God require it of thee, because it hath displeased thee? For thou beganst to speak, and not I: but if thou know any better thing, speak.

text.NRSV: Those who have sense will say to me, and the wise who hear me will say,
text.Rotherham: The men of mind, will say to me, yea any wise man hearkening unto me:—
text.KJV: Let men of understanding tell me, and let a wise man hearken unto me.
text.NABRE: Those who understand will say to me, all the wise who hear my views:
text.DRB: Let men of understanding speak to me, and let a wiseman hear me.

text.NRSV: ‘Job speaks without knowledge, his words are without insight.’
text.Rotherham: Job, without knowledge, doth speak, and, his words, are not with discretion.
text.KJV: Job hath spoken without knowledge, and his words were without wisdom.
text.NABRE: “Job speaks without knowledge, his words make no sense.
text.DRB: But Job hath spoken foolishly, and his words sound not discipline.

text.NRSV: Would that Job were tried to the limit, because his answers are those of the wicked.
text.Rotherham: Would that Job might be tested to the uttermost, for replying with the men of iniquity:
text.KJV: My desire is that Job may be tried unto the end because of his answers for wicked men.
text.NABRE: Let Job be tested to the limit, since his answers are those of the impious;
text.DRB: O my father, let Job be proved even to the end: cease not from the man of iniquity.

text.NRSV: For he adds rebellion to his sin; he claps his hands among us, and multiplies his words against God.”
text.Rotherham: For he addeth—unto his sin—rebellion, In our midst, he clappeth his hands, and multiplieth his sayings against GOD.
text.KJV: For he addeth rebellion unto his sin, he clappeth his hands among us, and multiplieth his words against God.
text.NABRE: For he is adding rebellion to his sin by brushing off our arguments and addressing many words to God.”
text.DRB: Who addeth blasphemy upon his sins, let him be restrained in the mean time among us: and then let him provoke God to judgment with his speeches.

text.NRSV: Elihu continued and said:
text.Rotherham: Moreover Elihu, responded and said:—
text.KJV: Elihu spake moreover, and said,
text.NABRE: Then Elihu answered and said:
text.DRB: Therefore Eliu again spake these words:

text.NRSV: “Do you think this to be just? You say, ‘I am in the right before God.’
text.Rotherham: This, dost thou think to be right? Thou hast said—My righteousness is more than GOD’S.
text.KJV: Thinkest thou this to be right, that thou saidst, My righteousness is more than God’s?
text.NABRE: Do you think it right to say, “I am in the right, not God”?
text.DRB: Doth thy cogitation seem just to thee, that thou saidst: I am juster than God?

text.NRSV: If you ask, ‘What advantage have I? How am I better off than if I had sinned?’
text.Rotherham: For thou dost say, How can one profit by thee? How can I benefit, more than by my sin?
text.KJV: For thou saidst, What advantage will it be unto thee? and, What profit shall I have, if I be cleansed from my sin?
text.NABRE: When you ask what it profits you, “What advantage do I have from not sinning?”
text.DRB: For thou saidst: That which is right doth not please thee: or what will it profit thee if I sin?

text.NRSV: I will answer you and your friends with you.
text.Rotherham: I, will answer thee plainly, and thy friends with thee.
text.KJV: I will answer thee, and thy companions with thee.
text.NABRE: I have words for a reply to you and your friends as well.
text.DRB: Therefore will I answer to thy words, and to thy friends with thee.

text.NRSV: Look at the heavens and see; observe the clouds, which are higher than you.
text.Rotherham: Look at the heavens and see,—and survey the skies—they are higher than thou.
text.KJV: Look unto the heavens, and see; and behold the clouds which are higher than thou.
text.NABRE: Look up to the skies and see; behold the heavens high above you.
text.DRB: Look up to heaven and see, and behold the sky, that it is higher than thou.

text.NRSV: If you have sinned, what do you accomplish against him? And if your transgressions are multiplied, what do you do to him?
text.Rotherham: If thou sinnest, what canst thou work against him? Or, if thy transgressions be multiplied, what canst thou do unto him?
text.KJV: If thou sinnest, what doest thou against him? or if thy transgressions be multiplied, what doest thou unto him?
text.NABRE: If you sin, what do you do to God? Even if your offenses are many, how do you affect him?
text.DRB: If thou sin, what shalt thou hurt him? And if thine iniquities be multiplied, what shall thou do against him?

text.NRSV: If you are righteous, what do you give to him; or what does he receive from your hand?
text.Rotherham: If thou art righteous, what canst thou give unto him? Or what, at thy hand, can he accept?
text.KJV: If thou be righteous, what givest thou him? or what receiveth he of thine hand?
text.NABRE: If you are righteous, what do you give him, or what does he receive from your hand?
text.DRB: Moreover if thou do justly, what shalt thou give him, or what shall he receive of thy hand?

text.NRSV: Your wickedness affects others like you, and your righteousness, other human beings.
text.Rotherham: Unto a man like thyself, might thy lawlessness [reach], and, unto a son of the earth-born, thy righteousness.
text.KJV: Thy wickedness may hurt a man as thou art; and thy righteousness may profit the son of man.
text.NABRE: Your wickedness affects only someone like yourself, and your justice, only a fellow human being.
text.DRB: Man that is like to thee, thy impiety shall hurt: and thy justice shall help the son of man.

text.NRSV: “Because of the multitude of oppressions people cry out; they call for help because of the arm of the mighty.
text.Rotherham: By reason of the multitude of oppressions, [men] make outcry, They cry for help, by reason of the arm of the mighty;
text.KJV: By reason of the multitude of oppressions they make the oppressed to cry: they cry out by reason of the arm of the mighty.
text.NABRE: In great oppression people cry out; they call for help because of the power of the great,
text.DRB: Because of the multitude of calumniators they shall cry: and shall wail for the force of the arm of tyrants.

text.NRSV: But no one says, ‘Where is God my Maker, who gives strength in the night,
text.Rotherham: But none saith—Where is GOD my maker, Who giveth songs in the night;
text.KJV: But none saith, Where is God my maker, who giveth songs in the night;
text.NABRE: No one says, “Where is God, my Maker, who gives songs in the night,
text.DRB: And he hath not said: Where is God, that made me, that hath given songs in the night?

text.NRSV: who teaches us more than the animals of the earth, and makes us wiser than the birds of the air?’
text.Rotherham: Who teacheth us more than the beasts of the earth, and, beyond the bird of the heavens, giveth us wisdom?
text.KJV: Who teacheth us more than the beasts of the earth, and maketh us wiser than the fowls of heaven?
text.NABRE: Teaches us more than the beasts of the earth, and makes us wiser than the birds of the heavens?”
text.DRB: Who teacheth us above the beasts of the earth, and instructeth us above the fowls of the air.

text.NRSV: There they cry out, but he does not answer, because of the pride of evildoers.
text.Rotherham: There, [men] make outcry, and he answereth not, because of the arrogance of evil-doers.
text.KJV: There they cry, but none giveth answer, because of the pride of evil men.
text.NABRE: Though thus they cry out, he does not answer because of the pride of the wicked.
text.DRB: There shall they cry, and he will not hear, because of the pride of the evil.

text.NRSV: Surely God does not hear an empty cry, nor does the Almighty regard it.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, vanity, will GOD not hear, Yea, the Almighty, will not regard it.
text.KJV: Surely God will not hear vanity, neither will the Almighty regard it.
text.NABRE: But it is idle to say God does not hear or that the Almighty does not take notice.
text.DRB: God therefore will not hear without cause, and the Omnipotent will behold the causes of every one.

text.NRSV: How much less when you say that you do not see him, that the case is before him, and you are waiting for him!
text.Rotherham: How much less when thou sayest thou wilt not regard him! The cause, is before him, and thou must wait for him.
text.KJV: Although thou sayest thou shalt not see him, yet judgment is before him; therefore trust thou in him.
text.NABRE: Even though you say, “You take no notice of it,” the case is before him; with trembling wait upon him.
text.DRB: Yea when thou shalt say: He considereth not: be judged before him, and expect him.

text.NRSV: And now, because his anger does not punish, and he does not greatly heed transgression,
text.Rotherham: But, now, because it is not so, [thou sayest] —His anger hath punished, and yet hath he not at all known of transgression;
text.KJV: But now, because it is not so, he hath visited in his anger; yet he knoweth it not in great extremity:
text.NABRE: But now that you have done otherwise, God’s anger punishes, nor does he show much concern over a life.
text.DRB: For he doth not now infer his fury, neither doth he revenge wickedness exceedingly.

text.NRSV: Job opens his mouth in empty talk, he multiplies words without knowledge.”
text.Rotherham: Thus, Job, vainly openeth his mouth, Without knowledge, he multiplieth words.
text.KJV: Therefore doth Job open his mouth in vain; he multiplieth words without knowledge.
text.NABRE: Yet Job to no purpose opens his mouth, multiplying words without knowledge.
text.DRB: Therefore Job in vain openeth his mouth, and without knowledge multiplieth words.

text.NRSV: Elihu continued and said:
text.Rotherham: And Elihu added and said:—
text.KJV: Elihu also proceeded, and said,
text.NABRE: Elihu continued and said:
text.DRB: Eliu also adding spake these words:

text.NRSV: “Bear with me a little, and I will show you, for I have yet something to say on God’s behalf.
text.Rotherham: Restrain thyself for me a little, and I will shew thee, that, yet—for GOD, there is justification,
text.KJV: Suffer me a little, and I will shew thee that I have yet to speak on God’s behalf.
text.NABRE: Wait a little and I will instruct you, for there are still words to be said for God.
text.DRB: Bear with me a little, and I will shew to thee: for as yet I have what to speak for God.

text.NRSV: I will fetch my knowledge from afar, and ascribe righteousness to my Maker.
text.Rotherham: I will bring my knowledge from afar, and, to my Maker, will I attribute righteousness.
text.KJV: I will fetch my knowledge from afar, and will ascribe righteousness to my Maker.
text.NABRE: I will assemble arguments from afar, and for my maker I will establish what is right.
text.DRB: I will repeat my knowledge from the beginning, and I will prove my maker just.

text.NRSV: For truly my words are not false; one who is perfect in knowledge is with you.
text.Rotherham: For, of a truth—not false, are my words, One of competent knowledge, is with thee.
text.KJV: For truly my words shall not be false: he that is perfect in knowledge is with thee.
text.NABRE: For indeed, my words are not a lie; one perfect in knowledge is before you.
text.DRB: For indeed my words are without lie, & perfect knowledge shall be proved to thee.

text.NRSV: “Surely God is mighty and does not despise any; he is mighty in strength of understanding.
text.Rotherham: Lo! GOD, is mighty, yet will he not despise, Mighty in vigour of mind;
text.KJV: Behold, God is mighty, and despiseth not any: he is mighty in strength and wisdom.
text.NABRE: Look, God is great, not disdainful; his strength of purpose is great. He does not preserve the life of the wicked.
text.DRB: God doth not cast away the mighty, whereas himself also is mighty.

text.NRSV: He does not keep the wicked alive, but gives the afflicted their right.
text.Rotherham: He will not keep alive one who is lawless, but, the right of oppressed ones, will he grant;
text.KJV: He preserveth not the life of the wicked: but giveth right to the poor.
text.NABRE: He establishes the right of the poor; he does not divert his eyes from the just
text.DRB: But he saveth not the impious, and he giveth judgment to the poor.

text.NRSV: He does not withdraw his eyes from the righteous, but with kings on the throne he sets them forever, and they are exalted.
text.Rotherham: He will not withdraw—from a righteous one—his eyes,—but, with kings on the throne, He hath seated men triumphantly, and they have been exalted.
text.KJV: He withdraweth not his eyes from the righteous: but with kings are they on the throne; yea, he doth establish them for ever, and they are exalted.
text.NABRE: But he seats them upon thrones with kings, exalted forever.
text.DRB: He shall not take away his eyes from the just man, and he placeth kings in the throne for ever, and there they are extolled.

text.NRSV: And if they are bound in fetters and caught in the cords of affliction,
text.Rotherham: But, if, bound in fetters, they have been captured with cords of affliction,
text.KJV: And if they be bound in fetters, and be holden in cords of affliction;
text.NABRE: If they are bound with fetters, held fast by bonds of affliction,
text.DRB: And if they shall be in chains, & be bound with the ropes of poverty.

text.NRSV: then he declares to them their work and their transgressions, that they are behaving proudly.
text.Rotherham: Then hath he declared to them their deed, and their transgressions—that they were wont to behave themselves proudly;
text.KJV: Then he sheweth them their work, and their transgressions that they have exceeded.
text.NABRE: He lets them know what they have done, and how arrogant are their sins.
text.DRB: He shall shew them their works, and their wicked deeds, because they have been violent.

text.NRSV: He opens their ears to instruction, and commands that they return from iniquity.
text.Rotherham: Thus hath he uncovered their ear to a warning, and said—that they should turn from iniquity.
text.KJV: He openeth also their ear to discipline, and commandeth that they return from iniquity.
text.NABRE: He opens their ears to correction and tells them to turn back from evil.
text.DRB: He also shall reveal their ear, to chastise them: & shall speak, that they may return from iniquity:

text.NRSV: If they listen and serve him, they complete their days in prosperity, and their years in pleasantness.
text.Rotherham: If they would hearken, and serve, they should complete their days, in prosperity, and their years, in pleasantness;
text.KJV: If they obey and serve him, they shall spend their days in prosperity, and their years in pleasures.
text.NABRE: If they listen and serve him, they spend their days in prosperity, their years in happiness.
text.DRB: If they shall hear and observe, they shall accomplish their days in good, and their years in glory.

text.NRSV: But if they do not listen, they shall perish by the sword, and die without knowledge.
text.Rotherham: But, if they would not hearken, by a weapon, should they pass away, and breathe their last, no one knowing.
text.KJV: But if they obey not, they shall perish by the sword, and they shall die without knowledge.
text.NABRE: But if they do not listen, they pass to the grave, they perish for lack of knowledge.
text.DRB: But if they hear not, they shall pass by the sword, and shall be consumed in folly.

text.NRSV: “The godless in heart cherish anger; they do not cry for help when he binds them.
text.Rotherham: Yea, the impious in heart, should store up anger, They should not cry for help, when he bound them.
text.KJV: But the hypocrites in heart heap up wrath: they cry not when he bindeth them.
text.NABRE: The impious in heart lay up anger; they do not cry for help when he binds them;
text.DRB: Dissemblers and crafty men provoke the wrath of God, neither shall they cry when they are bound.

text.NRSV: They die in their youth, and their life ends in shame.
text.Rotherham: Their soul should die in youth, and their life, among the unclean.
text.KJV: They die in youth, and their life is among the unclean.
text.NABRE: They will die young— their life among the reprobate.
text.DRB: Their soul shall die in tempest, and their life, among the effeminates.

text.NRSV: He delivers the afflicted by their affliction, and opens their ear by adversity.
text.Rotherham: He would deliver the humbled in his humiliation, and would uncover—in oppression—their ear.
text.KJV: He delivereth the poor in his affliction, and openeth their ears in oppression.
text.NABRE: But he saves the afflicted through their affliction, and opens their ears through oppression.
text.DRB: He shall deliver the poor out of his distress, and shall reveal his ear in tribulation.

text.NRSV: He also allured you out of distress into a broad place where there was no constraint, and what was set on your table was full of fatness.
text.Rotherham: Yea he might even have allured thee—out of the mouth of straitness, [into] a wide space—no narrowness there,—and, the food set down on thy table, should have been full of fatness.
text.KJV: Even so would he have removed thee out of the strait into a broad place, where there is no straitness; and that which should be set on thy table should be full of fatness.
text.NABRE: He entices you from distress, to a broad place without constraint; what rests on your table is rich food.
text.DRB: Therefore he shall save thee most largely out of the narrow mouth, and not having foundation under it: and the quietness of thy table shall be full of fatness.

text.NRSV: “But you are obsessed with the case of the wicked; judgment and justice seize you.
text.Rotherham: But, with the plea of a lawless one, thou art full, Plea and sentence, will take fast hold.
text.KJV: But thou hast fulfilled the judgment of the wicked: judgment and justice take hold on thee.
text.NABRE: Though you are full of the judgment of the wicked, judgment and justice will be maintained.
text.DRB: Thy cause is judged as an impious man's, cause and judgment thou shalt receive.

text.NRSV: Beware that wrath does not entice you into scoffing; and do not let the greatness of the ransom turn you aside.
text.Rotherham: Because there is wrath, [beware] lest he take thee away with a stroke, Then let not, a great ransom, mislead thee.
text.KJV: Because there is wrath, beware lest he take thee away with his stroke: then a great ransom cannot deliver thee.
text.NABRE: Let not anger at abundance entice you, nor great bribery lead you astray.
text.DRB: Let not therefore anger overcome thee, that thou oppress any man: neither let multitude of gifts incline thee.

text.NRSV: Will your cry avail to keep you from distress, or all the force of your strength?
text.Rotherham: Will he value thy riches? Nay not precious ore, nor all the forces of strength.
text.KJV: Will he esteem thy riches? no, not gold, nor all the forces of strength.
text.NABRE: Will your wealth equip you against distress, or all your exertions of strength?
text.DRB: Lay down thy greatness without tribulation, & all the puissant of strength.

text.NRSV: Do not long for the night, when peoples are cut off in their place.
text.Rotherham: Do not pant for the night, when peoples disappear from their place.
text.KJV: Desire not the night, when people are cut off in their place.
text.NABRE: Do not long for the night, when peoples vanish in their place.
text.DRB: Protract not the night, that peoples may come up for them.

text.NRSV: Beware! Do not turn to iniquity; for this you have chosen rather than affliction.
text.Rotherham: Beware, do not turn unto iniquity, For, this, thou hast chosen rather than affliction.
text.KJV: Take heed, regard not iniquity: for this hast thou chosen rather than affliction.
text.NABRE: Be careful; do not turn to evil; for this you have preferred to affliction.
text.DRB: Beware thou decline not to iniquity: for thou hast begun to follow it after misery.

text.NRSV: See, God is exalted in his power; who is a teacher like him?
text.Rotherham: Lo, GOD, exalteth himself by his strength, Who like him doth teach?
text.KJV: Behold, God exalteth by his power: who teacheth like him?
text.NABRE: Look, God is exalted in his power. What teacher is there like him?
text.DRB: Behold, God is high in his strength, and none is like to him among the law givers.

text.NRSV: Who has prescribed for him his way, or who can say, ‘You have done wrong’?
text.Rotherham: Who enjoined on him his way? and who ever said, Thou hast wrought perversity?
text.KJV: Who hath enjoined him his way? or who can say, Thou hast wrought iniquity?
text.NABRE: Who prescribes for him his way? Who says, “You have done wrong”?
text.DRB: Who can search his ways? Or who can say to him: Thou hast wrought iniquity?

text.NRSV: “Remember to extol his work, of which mortals have sung.
text.Rotherham: Remember, that thou extol his work, of which men have sung;
text.KJV: Remember that thou magnify his work, which men behold.
text.NABRE: Remember, you should extol his work, which people have praised in song.
text.DRB: Remember that thou knowest not his work, whereof men have sung.

text.NRSV: All people have looked on it; mortals gaze at it from afar.
text.Rotherham: Every son of earth, hath viewed it, Mortal man, looketh at it from afar.
text.KJV: Every man may see it; man may behold it afar off.
text.NABRE: All humankind beholds it; everyone views it from afar.
text.DRB: All men see him, every one beholdeth far off.

text.NRSV: Surely God is great, and we do not know him; the number of his years is unsearchable.
text.Rotherham: Lo, GOD, is greater than we can know, The number of his years, even past finding out!
text.KJV: Behold, God is great, and we know him not, neither can the number of his years be searched out.
text.NABRE: See, God is great beyond our knowledge, the number of his years past searching out.
text.DRB: Behold, God is great surmounting our knowledge: the number of his years is inestimable.

text.NRSV: For he draws up the drops of water; he distills his mist in rain,
text.Rotherham: For he draweth up drops of water, They trickle as rain through his mist;
text.KJV: For he maketh small the drops of water: they pour down rain according to the vapour thereof:
text.NABRE: He holds in check the waterdrops that filter in rain from his flood,
text.DRB: Who taketh away the drops of rain, & poureth out showers as it were gifts of water.

text.NRSV: which the skies pour down and drop upon mortals abundantly.
text.Rotherham: With which the clouds flow down, They drop on man in abundance.
text.KJV: Which the clouds do drop and distil upon man abundantly.
text.NABRE: Till the clouds flow with them and they rain down on all humankind.
text.DRB: Which flow out from the clouds, that cover all things from above.

text.NRSV: Can anyone understand the spreading of the clouds, the thunderings of his pavilion?
text.Rotherham: But surely none can understand the burstings of the cloud, the crashing of his pavilion!
text.KJV: Also can any understand the spreadings of the clouds, or the noise of his tabernacle?
text.NABRE: Can anyone understand the spreading clouds, the thunderings from his tent?
text.DRB: If he will stretch forth clouds as his tent,

text.NRSV: See, he scatters his lightning around him and covers the roots of the sea.
text.Rotherham: Lo! he hath spread out over it, his lightning, The bed of the sea, hath he covered.
text.KJV: Behold, he spreadeth his light upon it, and covereth the bottom of the sea.
text.NABRE: Look, he spreads his light over it, it covers the roots of the sea.
text.DRB: And lighten with his light from above, he shall cover also the ends of the sea.

text.NRSV: For by these he governs peoples; he gives food in abundance.
text.Rotherham: For, by those things, he executeth judgment on peoples, He giveth food in abundance:
text.KJV: For by them judgeth he the people; he giveth meat in abundance.
text.NABRE: For by these he judges the nations, and gives food in abundance.
text.DRB: For by these he judgeth peoples, and giveth victuals to many mortal men.

text.NRSV: He covers his hands with the lightning, and commands it to strike the mark.
text.Rotherham: Upon both hands, he putteth a covering of lightning, and layeth command upon it against an assailant:
text.KJV: With clouds he covereth the light; and commandeth it not to shine by the cloud that cometh betwixt.
text.NABRE: In his hands he holds the lightning, and he commands it to strike the mark.
text.DRB: In his hands he hideth the light, & commandeth it that it come again.

text.NRSV: Its crashing declares it; the cattle also, concerning the storm's coming.
text.Rotherham: His rolling thunder telleth concerning him,—The cattle, even, concerning him that is coming up.
text.KJV: The noise thereof sheweth concerning it, the cattle also concerning the vapour.
text.NABRE: His thunder announces him and incites the fury of the storm.
text.DRB: He sheweth his friend thereof, that it is his possession, and that he may ascend to it.

text.NRSV: “At this also my heart trembles, and leaps out of its place.
text.Rotherham: Yea, at this, my heart quaketh, and starteth up out of its place.
text.KJV: At this also my heart trembleth, and is moved out of his place.
text.NABRE: At this my heart trembles and leaps out of its place.
text.DRB: Upon this my heart is sore afraid, and is moved out of his place.

text.NRSV: Listen, listen to the thunder of his voice and the rumbling that comes from his mouth.
text.Rotherham: Hear! oh hear! the raging of his voice, A growling sound also, out of his mouth, goeth forth;
text.KJV: Hear attentively the noise of his voice, and the sound that goeth out of his mouth.
text.NABRE: Listen to his angry voice and the rumble that comes forth from his mouth!
text.DRB: Hear ye his speech in the terror of his voice, and the sound proceeding out of his mouth.

text.NRSV: Under the whole heaven he lets it loose, and his lightning to the corners of the earth.
text.Rotherham: Under the whole heavens, he letteth it loose, His lightning also, unto the wings of the earth;
text.KJV: He directeth it under the whole heaven, and his lightning unto the ends of the earth.
text.NABRE: Everywhere under the heavens he sends it, with his light, to the ends of the earth.
text.DRB: Under all the heavens he considereth, and his light is upon the ends of the earth.

text.NRSV: After it his voice roars; he thunders with his majestic voice, and he does not restrain the lightnings when his voice is heard.
text.Rotherham: After it, roareth a voice, He thundereth with his voice of majesty, Nor will he hold them back, when his voice is heard.
text.KJV: After it a voice roareth: he thundereth with the voice of his excellency; and he will not stay them when his voice is heard.
text.NABRE: Again his voice roars, his majestic voice thunders; he does not restrain them when his voice is heard.
text.DRB: After him shall sounding roar, he shall thunder with the voice of his greatness, and shall not be found out when his voice shall be heard.

text.NRSV: God thunders wondrously with his voice; he does great things that we cannot comprehend.
text.Rotherham: GOD thundereth with his voice, wonderfully, Doing great things, which we cannot know;
text.KJV: God thundereth marvellously with his voice; great things doeth he, which we cannot comprehend.
text.NABRE: God thunders forth marvels with his voice; he does great things beyond our knowing.
text.DRB: God shall thunder in his voice marvelously, he that doeth great & unsearchable things.

text.NRSV: For to the snow he says, ‘Fall on the earth’; and the shower of rain, his heavy shower of rain, is a strong force.
text.Rotherham: For, to the snow, he saith, Fall earthwards,—Also to the downpour of rain, yea the downpour of his mighty rains.
text.KJV: For he saith to the snow, Be thou on the earth; likewise to the small rain, and to the great rain of his strength.
text.NABRE: He says to the snow, “Fall to the earth”; likewise to his heavy, drenching rain.
text.DRB: He that commandeth the snow to descend upon the earth, and the winter rains, and the shower of his strength.

text.NRSV: He shuts up every human hand, so that everyone may know his work.
text.Rotherham: On the hand of every man, he setteth a seal, that all men may take note of his doing.
text.KJV: He sealeth up the hand of every man; that all men may know his work.
text.NABRE: He shuts up all humankind indoors, so that all people may know his work.
text.DRB: He that signeth in the hand of all men, that every one may know his works.

text.NRSV: Then the animals go into their lairs and remain in their dens.
text.Rotherham: So then the wild-beast hath gone into covert, and, in its lairs, doth it remain.
text.KJV: Then the beasts go into dens, and remain in their places.
text.NABRE: The wild beasts take to cover and remain quiet in their dens.
text.DRB: The beast shall enter into his covert, and shall abide in his den.

text.NRSV: From its chamber comes the whirlwind, and cold from the scattering winds.
text.Rotherham: Out of a chamber cometh a storm-wind, and, out of the north, cold.
text.KJV: Out of the south cometh the whirlwind: and cold out of the north.
text.NABRE: Out of its chamber the tempest comes forth; from the north winds, the cold.
text.DRB: From the inner parts shall a tempest come forth, & cold from Arcturus.

text.NRSV: By the breath of God ice is given, and the broad waters are frozen fast.
text.Rotherham: By the breath of GOD, is given—frost, and, the breadth of waters, is congealed;
text.KJV: By the breath of God frost is given: and the breadth of the waters is straitened.
text.NABRE: With his breath God brings the frost, and the broad waters congeal.
text.DRB: When God bloweth frost congealeth, and again waters are poured most largely.

text.NRSV: He loads the thick cloud with moisture; the clouds scatter his lightning.
text.Rotherham: Also, with moisture, burdeneth he the thick cloud, He disperseth his lightning-cloud;
text.KJV: Also by watering he wearieth the thick cloud: he scattereth his bright cloud:
text.NABRE: The clouds too are laden with moisture, the storm-cloud scatters its light.
text.DRB: Corn desireth clouds, & the clouds spread their light.

text.NRSV: They turn round and round by his guidance, to accomplish all that he commands them on the face of the habitable world.
text.Rotherham: Yea, the same, in circles, turneth itself to and fro, by his steering them to their work, whithersoever he commandeth them, over the face of the world, towards the earth.
text.KJV: And it is turned round about by his counsels: that they may do whatsoever he commandeth them upon the face of the world in the earth.
text.NABRE: He it is who changes their rounds, according to his plans, to do all that he commands them across the inhabited world.
text.DRB: Which go round about, whither-soever the will of the governer shall lead them, to all that he shall command them upon the face of the whole earth.

text.NRSV: Whether for correction, or for his land, or for love, he causes it to happen.
text.Rotherham: Whether, as a rod, or for his earth, or in lovingkindness, he causeth it to come.
text.KJV: He causeth it to come, whether for correction, or for his land, or for mercy.
text.NABRE: Whether for punishment or mercy, he makes it happen.
text.DRB: Whether in one tribe, or in his land, or in what place soever of his mercy he shall command them to be found.

text.NRSV: “Hear this, O Job; stop and consider the wondrous works of God.
text.Rotherham: Give thou ear unto this, O Job, Stay, and consider well the wonders of GOD:—
text.KJV: Hearken unto this, O Job: stand still, and consider the wondrous works of God.
text.NABRE: Listen to this, Job! Stand and consider the marvels of God!
text.DRB: Harken to these things Job: stand, and consider the marvels of God.

text.NRSV: Do you know how God lays his command upon them, and causes the lightning of his cloud to shine?
text.Rotherham: Canst thou got to know of GOD’S giving charge over them, or of the causing of the lightning of his cloud to shine forth?
text.KJV: Dost thou know when God disposed them, and caused the light of his cloud to shine?
text.NABRE: Do you know how God lays his command upon them, and makes the light shine forth from his clouds?
text.DRB: Doest thou know when God commanded the rains, that they shew the light of his clouds?

text.NRSV: Do you know the balancings of the clouds, the wondrous works of the one who is perfect in knowledge,
text.Rotherham: Canst thou get to know concerning the poisings of the thick cloud, the wonders of one who is perfect in knowledge?
text.KJV: Dost thou know the balancings of the clouds, the wondrous works of him which is perfect in knowledge?
text.NABRE: Do you know how the clouds are banked, the marvels of him who is perfect in knowledge?
text.DRB: Knowest thou the great paths of the clouds, and the perfect knowledges?

text.NRSV: you whose garments are hot when the earth is still because of the south wind?
text.Rotherham: That thy garments should be hot when he quieteth the earth from the south?
text.KJV: How thy garments are warm, when he quieteth the earth by the south wind?
text.NABRE: You, who swelter in your clothes when calm lies over the land from the south,
text.DRB: Are not thy garments hot, when the earth shall be blown with the South wind?

text.NRSV: Can you, like him, spread out the skies, hard as a molten mirror?
text.Rotherham: Didst thou spread out, with him, the skies, strong as a molten mirror?
text.KJV: Hast thou with him spread out the sky, which is strong, and as a molten looking glass?
text.NABRE: Can you with him spread out the firmament of the skies, hard as a molten mirror?
text.DRB: Thou perhaps madest the heavens with him, which are most sound, cast as it were of brass.

text.NRSV: Teach us what we shall say to him; we cannot draw up our case because of darkness.
text.Rotherham: Let us know what we shall say to him, We cannot set in order, by reason of darkness.
text.KJV: Teach us what we shall say unto him; for we cannot order our speech by reason of darkness.
text.NABRE: Teach us then what we shall say to him; we cannot, for the darkness, make our plea.
text.DRB: Shew us what we may say to him: for we are wrapped in darkness.

text.NRSV: Should he be told that I want to speak? Did anyone ever wish to be swallowed up?
text.Rotherham: Shall it be declared to him—that I would speak? Were any man to say aught, he might he destroyed?
text.KJV: Shall it be told him that I speak? if a man speak, surely he shall be swallowed up.
text.NABRE: Will he be told about it when I speak? Can anyone talk when he is being destroyed?
text.DRB: Who shall tell him the things that I speak? Yea if man shall speak, he shall be devoured.

text.NRSV: Now, no one can look on the light when it is bright in the skies, when the wind has passed and cleared them.
text.Rotherham: Yet, now, men see not the light, bright though it is in the skies, when, a wind, hath passed over, and cleansed them.
text.KJV: And now men see not the bright light which is in the clouds: but the wind passeth, and cleanseth them.
text.NABRE: Rather, it is as the light that cannot be seen while it is obscured by the clouds, till the wind comes by and sweeps them away.
text.DRB: But now they see not the light: suddenly the air shall be thickened into clouds, and the wind passing by shall drive them away.

text.NRSV: Out of the north comes golden splendor; around God is awesome majesty.
text.Rotherham: Out of the north, a golden light cometh, Upon GOD, is fearful splendour:
text.KJV: Fair weather cometh out of the north: with God is terrible majesty.
text.NABRE: From Zaphon the golden splendor comes, surrounding God’s awesome majesty!
text.DRB: From the North gold cometh, & toward God fearful praising.

text.NRSV: The Almighty—we cannot find him; he is great in power and justice, and abundant righteousness he will not violate.
text.Rotherham: The Almighty, whom we have not fully found out, is great in vigour,—Neither, justice nor abounding righteousness, will he weaken.
text.KJV: Touching the Almighty, we cannot find him out: he is excellent in power, and in judgment, and in plenty of justice: he will not afflict.
text.NABRE: The Almighty! We cannot find him, preeminent in power and judgment, abundant in justice, who never oppresses.
text.DRB: We cannot find him worthily: great of strength, and judgment, and justice, and he cannot be uttered.

text.NRSV: Therefore mortals fear him; he does not regard any who are wise in their own conceit.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore, do men revere him, He will not regard any who are wise in heart.
text.KJV: Men do therefore fear him: he respecteth not any that are wise of heart.
text.NABRE: Therefore people fear him; none can see him, however wise their hearts.
text.DRB: Therefore shall men fear him, and all that seem to themselves to be wise, shall not dare to behold him.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord answered Job out of the whirlwind:
text.Rotherham: Then Yahweh responded to Job, out of a storm, and said:—
text.KJV: Then the Lord answered Job out of the whirlwind, and said,
text.NABRE: Then the Lord answered Job out of the storm and said:
text.DRB: But our Lord answering Job out of a whirlwind, said:

text.NRSV: “Who is this that darkens counsel by words without knowledge?
text.Rotherham: Who is it that darkeneth counsel, by words, without knowledge?
text.KJV: Who is this that darkeneth counsel by words without knowledge?
text.NABRE: Who is this who darkens counsel with words of ignorance?
text.DRB: Who is this that wrappeth in sentences with unskillful words?

text.NRSV: Gird up your loins like a man, I will question you, and you shall declare to me.
text.Rotherham: Gird, I pray thee—like a strong man—thy loins, that I may ask thee, and inform thou me:
text.KJV: Gird up now thy loins like a man; for I will demand of thee, and answer thou me.
text.NABRE: Gird up your loins now, like a man; I will question you, and you tell me the answers!
text.DRB: Gird thy loins as a man: I will ask thee, and answer thou me.

text.NRSV: “Where were you when I laid the foundation of the earth? Tell me, if you have understanding.
text.Rotherham: Where wast thou, when I founded the earth? Tell, if thou knowest understanding!
text.KJV: Where wast thou when I laid the foundations of the earth? declare, if thou hast understanding.
text.NABRE: Where were you when I founded the earth? Tell me, if you have understanding.
text.DRB: Where wast thou when I laid the foundations of the earth? Tell me if thou hast understanding.

text.NRSV: Who determined its measurements—surely you know! Or who stretched the line upon it?
text.Rotherham: Who set the measurements thereof, if thou knowest? Or who stretched out over it a line?
text.KJV: Who hath laid the measures thereof, if thou knowest? or who hath stretched the line upon it?
text.NABRE: Who determined its size? Surely you know? Who stretched out the measuring line for it?
text.DRB: Who set the measures thereof, if thou know: or who stretched out the line upon it?

text.NRSV: On what were its bases sunk, or who laid its cornerstone
text.Rotherham: Whereon were the pedestals thereof sunk? Or who laid the corner stone thereof;—
text.KJV: Whereupon are the foundations thereof fastened? or who laid the corner stone thereof;
text.NABRE: Into what were its pedestals sunk, and who laid its cornerstone,
text.DRB: Upon what are the foundations thereof grounded? Or who let down the corner stone thereof,

text.NRSV: when the morning stars sang together and all the heavenly beings shouted for joy?
text.Rotherham: When the morning stars sang together, and all the sons of God shouted for joy?
text.KJV: When the morning stars sang together, and all the sons of God shouted for joy?
text.NABRE: While the morning stars sang together and all the sons of God shouted for joy?
text.DRB: when the morning stars praised me together, and all the sons of God made jubilation?

text.NRSV: “Or who shut in the sea with doors when it burst out from the womb?—
text.Rotherham: Or [who] shut in, with double doors, the sea, when, bursting out of the womb, it came forth;
text.KJV: Or who shut up the sea with doors, when it brake forth, as if it had issued out of the womb?
text.NABRE: Who shut within doors the sea, when it burst forth from the womb,
text.DRB: Who shut in the sea with doors when it brake forth, proceeding as it were out of a matrice:

text.NRSV: when I made the clouds its garment, and thick darkness its swaddling band,
text.Rotherham: When I put a cloud as the garment thereof, and a thick cloud as the swaddling-band thereof;
text.KJV: When I made the cloud the garment thereof, and thick darkness a swaddlingband for it,
text.NABRE: When I made the clouds its garment and thick darkness its swaddling bands?
text.DRB: When I made a cloud the garment thereof, and wrapped it in darkness as in clouts of infancy.

text.NRSV: and prescribed bounds for it, and set bars and doors,
text.Rotherham: And brake off for it my boundary, and fixed a bar and double doors;
text.KJV: And brake up for it my decreed place, and set bars and doors,
text.NABRE: When I set limits for it and fastened the bar of its door,
text.DRB: I compassed it with my bounds, and put bar and doors.

text.NRSV: and said, ‘Thus far shall you come, and no farther, and here shall your proud waves be stayed’?
text.Rotherham: And said—Hitherto, shalt thou come, and no further,—and, here, shalt thou set a limit to the majesty of thy waves?
text.KJV: And said, Hitherto shalt thou come, but no further: and here shall thy proud waves be stayed?
text.NABRE: And said: Thus far shall you come but no farther, and here shall your proud waves stop?
text.DRB: And I said. Hitherto thou shalt come, and shalt not proceed farther, and here thou shalt break thy swelling waves.

text.NRSV: “Have you commanded the morning since your days began, and caused the dawn to know its place,
text.Rotherham: Since thy days [began] hast thou commanded the morning? or caused the dawn to know its place;
text.KJV: Hast thou commanded the morning since thy days; and caused the dayspring to know his place;
text.NABRE: Have you ever in your lifetime commanded the morning and shown the dawn its place
text.DRB: Didst thou after thy birth command the morning, and shew the dawning his place?

text.NRSV: so that it might take hold of the skirts of the earth, and the wicked be shaken out of it?
text.Rotherham: That it might lay hold of the wings of the earth, and the lawless be shaken out of it?
text.KJV: That it might take hold of the ends of the earth, that the wicked might be shaken out of it?
text.NABRE: For taking hold of the ends of the earth, till the wicked are shaken from it?
text.DRB: And didst thou hold the extremities of the earth shaking them & hast thou shaken the impious out of it?

text.NRSV: It is changed like clay under the seal, and it is dyed like a garment.
text.Rotherham: It transformeth itself like the clay of a seal, so that things stand forth like one arrayed;
text.KJV: It is turned as clay to the seal; and they stand as a garment.
text.NABRE: The earth is changed as clay by the seal, and dyed like a garment;
text.DRB: The seal shall be restored as clay, and shall stand as a garment:

text.NRSV: Light is withheld from the wicked, and their uplifted arm is broken.
text.Rotherham: That their light may be withdrawn from the lawless, and, the lofty arm, be shivered.
text.KJV: And from the wicked their light is withholden, and the high arm shall be broken.
text.NABRE: But from the wicked their light is withheld, and the arm of pride is shattered.
text.DRB: From the impious their light shall be taken away, and the high arm shall be broken.

text.NRSV: “Have you entered into the springs of the sea, or walked in the recesses of the deep?
text.Rotherham: Hast thou entered as far as the springs of the sea? Or, through the secret recesses of the resounding deep, hast thou wandered?
text.KJV: Hast thou entered into the springs of the sea? or hast thou walked in the search of the depth?
text.NABRE: Have you entered into the sources of the sea, or walked about on the bottom of the deep?
text.DRB: Hast thou entered into the depths of the sea, and walked in the lowest parts of the great depth?

text.NRSV: Have the gates of death been revealed to you, or have you seen the gates of deep darkness?
text.Rotherham: Have the gates of death been disclosed to thee? And, the gates of the death-shade, couldst thou descry?
text.KJV: Have the gates of death been opened unto thee? or hast thou seen the doors of the shadow of death?
text.NABRE: Have the gates of death been shown to you, or have you seen the gates of darkness?
text.DRB: Have the gates of death been open to thee, & hast thou seen the darksome doors?

text.NRSV: Have you comprehended the expanse of the earth? Declare, if you know all this.
text.Rotherham: Hast thou well considered, even the breadths of the earth? Tell—if thou knowest it all!
text.KJV: Hast thou perceived the breadth of the earth? declare if thou knowest it all.
text.NABRE: Have you comprehended the breadth of the earth? Tell me, if you know it all.
text.DRB: Hast thou considered the breadth of the earth? Tell me if thou know all things,

text.NRSV: “Where is the way to the dwelling of light, and where is the place of darkness,
text.Rotherham: Where then is the way, the light shall abide? And, the darkness, where then is its place?
text.KJV: Where is the way where light dwelleth? and as for darkness, where is the place thereof,
text.NABRE: What is the way to the dwelling of light, and darkness—where is its place?
text.DRB: In what way the light dwelleth, & what is the place of darkness.

text.NRSV: that you may take it to its territory and that you may discern the paths to its home?
text.Rotherham: That thou mayest conduct it unto the bound thereof, and that thou mayest perceive the paths to its house.
text.KJV: That thou shouldest take it to the bound thereof, and that thou shouldest know the paths to the house thereof?
text.NABRE: That you may take it to its territory and know the paths to its home?
text.DRB: That thou canst bring every thing to his borders, & understand the paths of the house thereof.

text.NRSV: Surely you know, for you were born then, and the number of your days is great!
text.Rotherham: Thou knowest, for, then, hadst thou been born! And, in number, thy days are many!
text.KJV: Knowest thou it, because thou wast then born? or because the number of thy days is great?
text.NABRE: You know, because you were born then, and the number of your days is great!
text.DRB: Didst thou know then that thou shouldest be born? & didst thou know the number of thy days?

text.NRSV: “Have you entered the storehouses of the snow, or have you seen the storehouses of the hail,
text.Rotherham: Hast thou entered into the treasuries of the snow? And, the treasuries of the hail, couldst thou see?
text.KJV: Hast thou entered into the treasures of the snow? or hast thou seen the treasures of the hail,
text.NABRE: Have you entered the storehouses of the snow, and seen the storehouses of the hail
text.DRB: Hast thou entered into the treasures of the snow, or hast thou beheld treasures of hail?

text.NRSV: which I have reserved for the time of trouble, for the day of battle and war?
text.Rotherham: Which I have reserved for a time of distress, for the day of conflict and of war?
text.KJV: Which I have reserved against the time of trouble, against the day of battle and war?
text.NABRE: Which I have reserved for times of distress, for a day of war and battle?
text.DRB: Which I have prepared for the time of the enemy, for the day of fight & battle?

text.NRSV: What is the way to the place where the light is distributed, or where the east wind is scattered upon the earth?
text.Rotherham: Where then is the way the lightning is parted? The east wind spreadeth itself abroad over the earth.
text.KJV: By what way is the light parted, which scattereth the east wind upon the earth?
text.NABRE: What is the way to the parting of the winds, where the east wind spreads over the earth?
text.DRB: What way is the light spread, is heat divided upon the earth?

text.NRSV: “Who has cut a channel for the torrents of rain, and a way for the thunderbolt,
text.Rotherham: Who hath cloven—for the torrent—a channel? Or a way for the lightning of thunders;
text.KJV: Who hath divided a watercourse for the overflowing of waters, or a way for the lightning of thunder;
text.NABRE: Who has laid out a channel for the downpour and a path for the thunderstorm
text.DRB: Who gave course to the most vehement shower, and the way of the sounding thunder:

text.NRSV: to bring rain on a land where no one lives, on the desert, which is empty of human life,
text.Rotherham: To give rain over the no-man’s land, the desert, where no son of earth is;
text.KJV: To cause it to rain on the earth, where no man is; on the wilderness, wherein there is no man;
text.NABRE: To bring rain to uninhabited land, the unpeopled wilderness;
text.DRB: That it should rain upon the earth, without man in the desert, where no mortal man abideth:

text.NRSV: to satisfy the waste and desolate land, and to make the ground put forth grass?
text.Rotherham: To satisfy the wild and the wilderness, to cause to spring forth the meadow of young grass?
text.KJV: To satisfy the desolate and waste ground; and to cause the bud of the tender herb to spring forth?
text.NABRE: To drench the desolate wasteland till the desert blooms with verdure?
text.DRB: That it should fill the desert and desolate ground, and should bring forth green grass?

text.NRSV: “Has the rain a father, or who has begotten the drops of dew?
text.Rotherham: Hath the rain a father? Or who hath begotten the drops of dew?
text.KJV: Hath the rain a father? or who hath begotten the drops of dew?
text.NABRE: Has the rain a father? Who has begotten the drops of dew?
text.DRB: Who is the father of rain? Or who begot the drops of dew?

text.NRSV: From whose womb did the ice come forth, and who has given birth to the hoarfrost of heaven?
text.Rotherham: Out of whose womb, came forth the ice? And, the hoar-frost of the heavens, who hath given it birth?
text.KJV: Out of whose womb came the ice? and the hoary frost of heaven, who hath gendered it?
text.NABRE: Out of whose womb comes the ice, and who gives the hoarfrost its birth in the skies,
text.DRB: Out of whose womb came forth ice? And frost from heaven who engendered?

text.NRSV: The waters become hard like stone, and the face of the deep is frozen.
text.Rotherham: Like a stone, are the waters congealed, and, the face of the roaring deep, becometh firm!
text.KJV: The waters are hid as with a stone, and the face of the deep is frozen.
text.NABRE: When the waters lie covered as though with stone that holds captive the surface of the deep?
text.DRB: Waters are hardened like stone, and the face of the depth is congealed.

text.NRSV: “Can you bind the chains of the Pleiades, or loose the cords of Orion?
text.Rotherham: Canst thou bind the fetters of the Pleiades? Or, the bands of Orion, canst thou unloose?
text.KJV: Canst thou bind the sweet influences of Pleiades, or loose the bands of Orion?
text.NABRE: Have you tied cords to the Pleiades, or loosened the bonds of Orion?
text.DRB: Shalt thou be able to join together the shining stars Pleiades, or canst thou dissipate the circuit of Arcturus?

text.NRSV: Can you lead forth the Mazzaroth in their season, or can you guide the Bear with its children?
text.Rotherham: Canst thou bring forth the signs of the Zodiac each in its season? Or, the Bear and her Young, canst thou lead?
text.KJV: Canst thou bring forth Mazzaroth in his season? or canst thou guide Arcturus with his sons?
text.NABRE: Can you bring forth the Mazzaroth in their season, or guide the Bear with her children?
text.DRB: Doest thou bring forth the day star in his time, & make the evening star to rise upon the children of the earth?

text.NRSV: Do you know the ordinances of the heavens? Can you establish their rule on the earth?
text.Rotherham: Knowest thou, the statutes of the heavens? Or didst thou appoint his dominion over the earth?
text.KJV: Knowest thou the ordinances of heaven? canst thou set the dominion thereof in the earth?
text.NABRE: Do you know the ordinances of the heavens; can you put into effect their plan on the earth?
text.DRB: Doest thou know the order of heaven, and shalt thou put down the reason thereof on the earth?

text.NRSV: “Can you lift up your voice to the clouds, so that a flood of waters may cover you?
text.Rotherham: Canst thou lift up, to the thick cloud, thy voice, and the overflow of waters cover thee?
text.KJV: Canst thou lift up thy voice to the clouds, that abundance of waters may cover thee?
text.NABRE: Can you raise your voice to the clouds, for them to cover you with a deluge of waters?
text.DRB: Shalt thou elevate the voice in the cloud, & the violence of the waters cover thee?

text.NRSV: Can you send forth lightnings, so that they may go and say to you, ‘Here we are’?
text.Rotherham: Canst thou send forth the lightnings, so that they go, and say to thee, Behold us?
text.KJV: Canst thou send lightnings, that they may go, and say unto thee, Here we are?
text.NABRE: Can you send forth the lightnings on their way, so that they say to you, “Here we are”?
text.DRB: Shalt thou send lightnings, and will they go, and returning shall they say to thee: Here we are?

text.NRSV: Who has put wisdom in the inward parts, or given understanding to the mind?
text.Rotherham: Who hath put—into cloud-forms—wisdom? Or who hath given—to the meteor—understanding?
text.KJV: Who hath put wisdom in the inward parts? or who hath given understanding to the heart?
text.NABRE: Who gives wisdom to the ibis, and gives the rooster understanding?
text.DRB: Who put wisdom in the heart of man or who gave the cock understanding?

text.NRSV: Who has the wisdom to number the clouds? Or who can tilt the waterskins of the heavens,
text.Rotherham: Who can count the thin clouds, in wisdom? And, the bottles of the heavens, who can empty out;
text.KJV: Who can number the clouds in wisdom? or who can stay the bottles of heaven,
text.NABRE: Who counts the clouds with wisdom? Who tilts the water jars of heaven
text.DRB: Who shall declare the manner of the heavens, and the harmony of heaven who shall make to sleep?

text.NRSV: when the dust runs into a mass and the clods cling together?
text.Rotherham: When the dust is cast into a clod, and the lumps are bound together?
text.KJV: When the dust groweth into hardness, and the clods cleave fast together?
text.NABRE: So that the dust of earth is fused into a mass and its clods stick together?
text.DRB: When was the dust poured on the earth, and the clods compact together?

text.NRSV: “Can you hunt the prey for the lion, or satisfy the appetite of the young lions,
text.Rotherham: Wilt thou hunt—for the Lioness—prey? Or, the craving of the Strong Lion, wilt thou satisfy;
text.KJV: Wilt thou hunt the prey for the lion? or fill the appetite of the young lions,
text.NABRE: Do you hunt the prey for the lion or appease the hunger of young lions,
text.DRB: Shalt thou take a prey for the lioness and fill the appetite of her whelps?

text.NRSV: when they crouch in their dens, or lie in wait in their covert?
text.Rotherham: When they settle down in dens, abide in covert, for lying in wait?
text.KJV: When they couch in their dens, and abide in the covert to lie in wait?
text.NABRE: While they crouch in their dens, or lie in ambush in the thicket?
text.DRB: When they lie in the dens, & in holes sit in wait?

text.NRSV: Who provides for the raven its prey, when its young ones cry to God, and wander about for lack of food?
text.Rotherham: Who prepareth for the Raven his nourishment,—when his young ones—unto GOD—cry out, [when] they wander for lack of food?
text.KJV: Who provideth for the raven his food? when his young ones cry unto God, they wander for lack of meat.
text.NABRE: Who provides nourishment for the raven when its young cry out to God, wandering about without food?
text.DRB: Who prepareth for the raven her meat, when her young ones cry to God, wandering about, because they have not meat?

text.NRSV: “Do you know when the mountain goats give birth? Do you observe the calving of the deer?
text.Rotherham: Knowest thou the season when the Wild Goats of the crags beget? The bringing forth of the hinds, canst thou observe?
text.KJV: Knowest thou the time when the wild goats of the rock bring forth? or canst thou mark when the hinds do calve?
text.NABRE: Do you know when mountain goats are born, or watch for the birth pangs of deer,
text.DRB: Hast thou known the time when the wild goats bring forth young among the rocks, or hast thou observed the hinds when they fawn?

text.NRSV: Can you number the months that they fulfill, and do you know the time when they give birth,
text.Rotherham: Canst thou count the months they fulfil? Or knowest thou the time when they give birth?
text.KJV: Canst thou number the months that they fulfil? or knowest thou the time when they bring forth?
text.NABRE: Number the months that they must fulfill, or know when they give birth,
text.DRB: Hast thou numbered the months of their conceiving, and known the time of their bearing?

text.NRSV: when they crouch to give birth to their offspring, and are delivered of their young?
text.Rotherham: They kneel down, their young, they bring forth; their pains, they throw off;
text.KJV: They bow themselves, they bring forth their young ones, they cast out their sorrows.
text.NABRE: When they crouch down and drop their young, when they deliver their progeny?
text.DRB: They bow down themselves to bring forth young, and they cast them, and make roarings.

text.NRSV: Their young ones become strong, they grow up in the open; they go forth, and do not return to them.
text.Rotherham: Their young become strong, they grow up in the open field, they go out, and return not unto them.
text.KJV: Their young ones are in good liking, they grow up with corn; they go forth, and return not unto them.
text.NABRE: Their offspring thrive and grow in the open, they leave and do not return.
text.DRB: Their young are separated, and go to feed: they go forth, and return not to them.

text.NRSV: “Who has let the wild donkey go free? Who has loosed the bonds of the swift donkey,
text.Rotherham: Who hath sent forth the Wild Ass free? And, the bands of the swift-runner, who hath loosed?
text.KJV: Who hath sent out the wild ass free? or who hath loosed the bands of the wild ass?
text.NABRE: Who has given the wild donkey his freedom, and who has loosed the wild ass from bonds?
text.DRB: Who hath dismist the wild ass free, and who hath loosed his bonds?
